206856.explanation: In our city, we are working towards a new economy for a healthy planet. We focus on an innovative and clean industry. GroenLinks-PvdA also clearly chooses to say goodbye to the old, fossil industry. This ensures sustainable employment.

206857.explanation: The Brainport economy must be in balance with people and nature. That is why GroenLinks-PvdA invests in housing, care, education, and green spaces. The business community must also make a fair contribution. Choosing new high-tech companies means, for us, also saying farewell to the fossil industry.

206858.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA focuses on a safe and sustainable energy transition. Space in Eindhoven is limited. We prefer to invest in rooftop solar panels, district heating, and insulating homes. That is cleaner, smarter, and safer.

206859.explanation: Of course, elderly people should have a say in municipal policy, just like other groups in our society. Participation can take many forms, such as a citizens' assembly in which all groups are represented.

206860.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to give residents more direct influence on city governance. The first citizens' assembly is already in the works. We want to have more.

206861.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to give residents more direct influence on city governance. We prefer this to happen beforehand, via a citizens' assembly, not afterwards through a referendum.

206862.explanation: For years, GroenLinks-PvdA has subsidized activities in neighbourhoods. After all, residents know best what is needed for livability. We want these subsidies to continue. Schemes that work well in Pact Woensel Zuid will be expanded to the rest of the city.

206863.explanation: It is important that Eindhoven remains affordable for everyone. That is why we keep housing costs manageable. GroenLinks-PvdA ensures that those who can bear the most shoulder the heaviest financial burden.

206864.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA works with ambition and great commitment to stop the climate crisis and make Eindhoven sustainable. This is a demanding and challenging task. We also expect this commitment from companies that establish themselves in the city. That is why we prioritize sustainable businesses.

206865.explanation: Our region is well-known as an attractive location for companies. We owe this to our favourable business climate; it is good to work, live, and recreate in our green city. GroenLinks-PvdA prefers to invest in measures that maintain this. No promotional campaign can match that.

206866.explanation: Philips has given a lot to Eindhoven and is always welcome here. However, the company itself chose to relocate its headquarters. For a strong local economy, the municipality is therefore better off investing in making current companies more sustainable.

206867.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA, like many residents, has a big heart for our four-legged friends. Owning dogs entails costs. Therefore, this tax cannot simply be abolished.

206868.explanation: The local entrepreneur is the linchpin in a neighbourhood. GroenLinks-PvdA therefore supports these local entrepreneurs so they are not pushed out by large multinationals. We give them the freedom to determine their opening hours themselves and continue dialogue if they want to change those hours.

206869.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants a ban on misleading advertisements for polluting products. Tobacco advertisements are already prohibited; we want to extend this to fossil fuel and gambling ads. We want to promote healthy and sustainable alternatives instead.

206870.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to provide humane reception for people fleeing war and violence. We choose to distribute the reception fairly across the city. Good distribution also benefits integration.

206871.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to help people in need, whether a single mother or a status holder. If someone has a residence permit, they should get a good start and not have to wait years in reception centers.

206872.explanation: Integration is investment. GroenLinks-PvdA believes refugees, family migrants, labor migrants, and expats deserve a fair chance to participate from day one. By investing in language and guidance towards (volunteer) work, we ensure good integration.

206873.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to provide humane reception for people fleeing war and violence. We believe Eindhoven has a duty to make a significant contribution to this, especially when others fall short.

206874.explanation: The colonial and slavery past has caused much suffering. Its effects continue to this day. GroenLinks-PvdA waits for the municipal research but believes the municipality should be willing to offer apologies.

206875.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports an inclusive society. A standard quiet room requires a large investment. When there is a demand for a prayer room in municipal buildings, we naturally want to enable this.

206876.explanation: Labor migrants sometimes face poor housing, low wages, and bad working conditions. GroenLinks-PvdA does not accept this. That is why we demand good housing and fair wages from employers.

206877.explanation: Residents living near industrial estates have a right to clean and livable neighborhoods. GroenLinks-PvdA therefore wants the fossil and polluting industry to leave De Hurk and Eindhoven. We are willing to spend money to achieve this.

206878.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA is very proud of the greening of Eindhoven. We want more residents to enjoy this. Therefore, we make it easier and support good initiatives financially. Think of green or blue roofs, rain barrels, and the Tegeltaxi.

206879.explanation: Animals deserve respectful treatment. GroenLinks-PvdA therefore supports withdrawing fishing rights. We must be honest: much of the fishing water in our city is not under the municipality's responsibility.

206880.explanation: Thanks to many night enthusiasts, our nightlife is more vibrant than ever. As in previous years, GroenLinks-PvdA accommodates innovative night initiatives and new (pop-up) locations, always with attention to local residents.

206881.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA is proud of our variety of events: from carnival to Hit the City. We make it easier to organize neighborhood activities or events, from small street parties to larger initiatives.

206882.explanation: A sustainable and clean economy is necessary for our climate and a healthy living environment. GroenLinks-PvdA has already established a fund in Eindhoven for this transition. We always welcome more opportunities.

206883.explanation: Precisely to protect our heritage, we must counteract climate change. With respect for our heritage, we must look more broadly at what is possible. For example, how solar panels on roofs can still be feasible.

206884.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to stop the climate crisis. We do this primarily by placing solar panels on as many roofs as possible and through our sustainable Eindhoven Energy Company. We do not exclude ground-mounted solar panels.

206885.explanation: Every resident of Eindhoven should be able to play sports. It is healthy and fun. That is why GroenLinks-PvdA makes sports affordable and accessible for everyone.

206886.explanation: As GroenLinks-PvdA, we have successfully campaigned for more libraries in neighborhoods. It is a pleasant place to meet other people and learn new things. We will continue opening new neighborhood libraries.

206887.explanation: Everyone is welcome in the library. To read a book, study, take a course, or just have a cup of coffee. That is why GroenLinks-PvdA makes the library free for everyone.

206888.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA considers a strong community life and social organizations very important for our city. We support them by helping with good and affordable spaces.

206889.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes the rich history of Eindhoven deserves an important place. We have allocated a lot of money in recent years. We would like to continue this.

206890.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA chooses familiar faces in every neighborhood. This ensures more safety, good prevention, and social cohesion.

206891.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA chooses good enforcement in every neighborhood. We therefore welcome the possibility of deploying BOAs 24 hours a day. When necessary, we will use this in consultation with the police.

206892.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA stands for an inclusive society where everyone can participate as much as possible. By making BOAs more inclusive, we enrich and strengthen municipal enforcement.

206893.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants good cooperation between police and BOAs. BOAs should not take over police tasks but be a familiar face in the neighborhoods. When things threaten to go wrong, the police take over.

206894.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA prefers to invest in neighborhood officers who know the area. Better a familiar face on the street than large-scale camera surveillance.

206896.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA invests heavily in housing construction. We consider all possible options for affordable and good housing.

206897.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to build more suitable homes for the elderly in their own neighborhood. By giving priority and help with moving, we ensure flow. This frees up homes for families and starters.

206898.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA invests heavily in housing for all ages: from students to seniors. We mainly build life-course resilient homes so people over 50 can also live here without having to move.

206899.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA invests heavily in housing. We believe a priority scheme should be possible. The municipality must legally substantiate this well. In urgent cases, for example during a divorce or illness, this should definitely be an option.

206900.explanation: There are huge waiting lists for social housing. GroenLinks-PvdA therefore invests heavily in social rental and owner-occupied homes. This way, young people can finally have a home of their own.

206901.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA stands for living in a livable city. Parks, green streets, and the city forest are extremely important for this. By densifying, we preserve space for greenery.

206903.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA prefers to invest in people to support them and help them find work. Many people on social assistance want to make a positive contribution to our city. By trusting instead of distrusting, scandals like the childcare benefits affair are prevented.

206904.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to take a big step to help people in debt. We take Arnhem’s approach as an example by buying up debts and forgiving them.

206907.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that people with low incomes should continue to travel for free on public transport through the minimaregeling (minimum scheme).

206908.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA stands for an accessible city. We ensure that buildings, playgrounds, and public spaces are accessible and that experts by experience have a say in what is needed.

206910.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA gladly continues making our city center more car-free. We will make the entire Kleine Berg, Emmasingel, Vestdijk, Stationsweg, and Kruisstraat car-free. This creates more space for squares, cyclists, pedestrians, and greenery.

206911.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports a tram in our growing city. A tram is faster, more sustainable, and can carry more passengers comfortably and affordably.

206912.explanation: The ring road is currently too dangerous for cyclists and pedestrians. GroenLinks-PvdA therefore wants to reduce the speed limit to 50 km/h. This is also good for air quality.

206913.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants children to be able to play safely on the street and for the air to be cleaner. For this, a maximum speed of 30 km/h is essential. We want the municipality to enforce this.

206914.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to stop the climate crisis. We do this by building sustainably and climate-resiliently and by adding greenery and water for cooling. This is the best way to make homes cooler and combat heat stress.

206915.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA has great appreciation for informal caregivers in our city. We support informal caregivers and help them where needed.

206856.explanation: Our economy must remain strong and diverse (not too dependent on ASML). For this reason, we invest extra in maintaining and expanding our existing major companies Philips and Thermo Fisher and invest in developing new tech startups.

206857.explanation: ASML is a unique high-tech company with worldwide strategic significance. It provides a lot of employment. At the same time, we must also ensure good accessibility, housing, and facilities, with companies also contributing financially.

206858.explanation: Small nuclear reactors can provide electricity day and night, even when there is no wind or sun. Space is scarce in our city, but we are positive about this development somewhere in the Brainport region and are willing to support it.

206859.explanation: As far as we are concerned, there should be periodic consultations between the municipality and senior organizations about housing, care, and the welfare of seniors in the municipality.

206860.explanation: A citizens' assembly forms a reflection of society. Residents come together to find the best solutions to a problem. It gives residents influence, leads to better-considered choices and broader support.

206861.explanation: Complex issues are reduced to a simple yes/no in referendums. We prefer citizens' assemblies in which residents talk together and arrive at well-founded solutions with broad support.

206862.explanation: Residents know what is happening in the neighborhood and what is really needed. With a neighborhood budget, they determine what needs to be done. We pay attention to all neighborhoods, including those doing well. In case of problems or extra requests, the neighborhood budget can be temporarily increased.

206863.explanation: The CDA ensured that local taxes remained low during 2022-2026. We will also protect the wallets of our residents and businesses in the coming period 2026-2030. Local taxes may not grow faster than inflation and will never exceed the national average.

206864.explanation: Although we promote sustainability, this goes too far and is unfeasible in practice. We need many types of businesses in Eindhoven. However, we do give priority to sustainable companies when allocating land.

206865.explanation: We carry out targeted actions for those companies that strengthen our economy and society.

206866.explanation: When you say Eindhoven, you say Philips. Together with the High Tech Campus, we are developing a concrete proposal to bring Philips’ headquarters and/or other business units back to Eindhoven. Because Philips provides research, innovations, and employment. And that is good for our city.

206867.explanation: We want to use the dog tax for well-maintained dog walking fields and sufficient trash bins.

206868.explanation: It is up to local entrepreneurs themselves to decide whether they want to open in the evening or on Sundays. General opening hours in Eindhoven have already been expanded for all entrepreneurs in recent years; we will not extend them further.

206869.explanation: We consider this a symbolic measure that has little effect. Instead of prohibiting something, we prefer to stimulate the use of sustainable goods.

206870.explanation: We support small-scale and dispersed shelter instead of large shelter locations. This reduces pressure on the neighborhood, causes less nuisance, shelters are better manageable, and integration proceeds more smoothly.

206871.explanation: There are also many other groups of residents who need social rental housing and have been on a waiting list for a long time. Think of residents from Eindhoven or certain professional groups we need in our city and who should sometimes get priority when allocating a home.

206872.explanation: We make it clear to all newcomers (labor migrants, refugees, and expats) “how we do things in Eindhoven,” what their duties and responsibilities are, and where to ask for help or information. The goal is participation and contributing from day one.

206873.explanation: When asylum seekers are in the Netherlands, we distribute them fairly across all municipalities. If the national inflow decreases (which we believe is necessary), the shelter capacity in Eindhoven will also decrease.

206874.explanation: Apologies are not necessary. Research shows there is no indication that the city government at that time had a prominent or structural role in the colonial past.

206875.explanation: We do not want to impose this obligatorily. That goes too far.

206876.explanation: Otherwise, we often see that nuisance arises. Exploitation of labor migrants is unacceptable, and therefore we hold employers responsible for providing adequate space, privacy, safety, and reasonable rent.

206877.explanation: Relocating simply shifts the nuisance and is not realistic in the short term. What companies must do is take measures to reduce both odor nuisance and emissions. We do this by investing in cleaner technologies, so that the livability in 't Ven improves.

206878.explanation: A budget is available for every neighborhood to improve the district. For example, if residents want to green their area, this can be funded from that budget.

206879.explanation: Fishing should be and remain possible.

206880.explanation: We support a vibrant nightlife and initiatives that enhance it. Of course, we act immediately when there is nuisance.

206881.explanation: We want to be a dynamic and lively city. Festivals and parties are part of that, as long as they happen in a respectful and safe manner and the livability is maintained.

206882.explanation: This way, we encourage and assist companies in taking a sustainable step.

206883.explanation: Monuments must also be sustainably maintained with care. Current regulations often make it unnecessarily difficult to implement energy measures.

206884.explanation: Space in Eindhoven is too limited for large solar parks. We much prefer to encourage the use of all available roofs for solar energy.

206885.explanation: This is already done through the Youth and Adult Fund. We will continue this. Sports must be accessible to everyone, including people with low incomes.

206886.explanation: A bibliotoop (community library) in every district is useful because everyone can read, learn, and get help with digital matters close to home. It also makes it easier for neighborhood residents to meet each other.

206887.explanation: The library must be accessible so that everyone, including those with little money, can borrow books and get help with learning or digital matters. This ensures everyone can participate, regardless of income or background.

206888.explanation: This better utilizes the buildings and creates more space to come together and organize activities. There are already many great examples in our city. We will continue to encourage this, provided it is, of course, safe and feasible.

206889.explanation: Eindhoven does not have many monuments. Therefore, we need to be extra careful with what we still have.

206890.explanation: Every neighborhood should have its own district enforcement officer (boa) who is visible and approachable. This is not yet the case. Boa's are needed to address nuisance and minor annoyances in the neighborhood.

206891.explanation: Currently, this is only on weekends and in the city center, but livability problems also occur during the week, in neighborhoods, and at night.

206892.explanation: We want boa's to be visibly neutral. We regulate this with a uniform. A clear religious expression does not fit the role of an authority figure.

206893.explanation: Enforcement officers should be well-trained and equipped with the right tools, including at least body cameras and handcuffs. If a baton is desirable for the safety of boa's, we are open to discussing this under the right conditions (for example, for a specific group of boa's).

206894.explanation: We deploy camera surveillance more often and more quickly in a targeted way. We will expand the number of movable cameras to deploy them at unsafe situations in neighborhoods. In the city center, we will expand camera surveillance to avoid 'blind spots' and ensure criminals are not lost from sight.

206896.explanation: We do not consider this a municipal task. Developers and housing corporations must ensure enough affordable homes are built.

206897.explanation: We make it attractive for seniors to move on to a new affordable home, freeing up their (larger) houses for families with children.

206898.explanation: Building additional homes for seniors is crucial for turnover. By making it appealing for seniors to relocate to a new affordable home, (larger) houses become available for starters and families.

206899.explanation: We believe residents of Eindhoven should have priority when allocating a home. This is where you feel at home. Specific agreements are already being made with developers, but this will also apply to housing corporations.

206900.explanation: We want young people to be able to build their future in Eindhoven without having to leave because they cannot find affordable housing.

206901.explanation: We believe that ‘building in green areas’ is unnecessarily seen as taboo, as well-integrated housing can go hand in hand with greenery. Moreover, when assessing housing plans, we should focus not on the amount of 'green' on the map but on the actual quality of that greenery.

206903.explanation: We enforce against unjustified benefits and therefore increase our efforts on enforcement to the level of comparable large cities. Residents who make an administrative error in their social assistance application will, of course, not be immediately penalized.

206904.explanation: It is more helpful to guide people properly, detect problems early, and make a plan together, instead of simply writing off debts.

206907.explanation: We have had free bus travel for low-income residents for years and will continue this, so they can also use the bus.

206908.explanation: If there are specific signals that there are too few parking spaces for people with disabilities in a neighborhood, we will naturally look into it favorably. We currently have no signals that there are too few parking spaces for people with disabilities.

206910.explanation: More space will be created for greenery, squares, and parks in the city center, but all destinations in the city must remain accessible for residents and entrepreneurs with cars. Moreover, the ring road must be brought in order to ensure traffic flows smoothly.

206911.explanation: A tram is not realistic or profitable at this time. We prefer to invest the money in buses and trains that run frequently and are affordable.

206912.explanation: The speed limit between John F. Kennedylaan and Eisenhowerlaan remains 70 km/h. In other parts of the ring road, the speed can be lowered if it improves traffic safety and flow.

206913.explanation: This improves traffic safety in the neighborhood. We have implemented this in many streets recently and will continue to do so.

206914.explanation: We do not see this as a municipal task.

206915.explanation: We work with a reduced parking rate for family and friends visiting you, entrepreneurs, childcare and education, healthcare personnel, and visitors to social and volunteer organizations. We will further expand the 'visitor scheme' in terms of hours.

206856.explanation: We find it important that the Brainport region continues to develop. Innovative tech companies are needed for this. We want a good mix of different companies in our region.

206857.explanation: ASML is currently the engine of the regional economy. In addition, they take their social responsibility, just as Philips did a century ago.

206858.explanation: We cannot manage with only solar and wind energy. Nuclear energy is one of the solutions for the energy shortage. Small nuclear power plants are important for relieving the energy grid.

206859.explanation: Everyone counts. Seniors are also represented in the city council.

206860.explanation: Political parties represent the voices of Eindhoven residents. Through participation, residents must be able to engage in dialogue and contribute ideas.

206861.explanation: Political parties represent the voices of Eindhoven residents. Through participation, residents must be able to engage in dialogue and contribute ideas.

206862.explanation: There are many new residents in Eindhoven. It is especially on the street and neighborhood level that people can meet each other. The municipality supports initiatives where residents improve their own neighborhood.

206863.explanation: The expenses for homeowners in Eindhoven are already high. The VVD does not want to increase these charges any further.

206864.explanation: All businesses are welcome as long as they comply with laws and regulations.

206865.explanation: Any form of positive promotion for Eindhoven is welcome.

206866.explanation: It is not up to the municipality to decide where any company has its headquarters. Eindhoven must ensure itself that we are as attractive as possible.

206867.explanation: The VVD has long wanted to abolish the dog tax. Other pets are also not taxed. Furthermore, the dog tax is not spent on the dog owner.

206868.explanation: The VVD encourages freedom for entrepreneurs, as long as they do not cause nuisance to the residents.

206869.explanation: Residents are fully capable of making their own choices.

206870.explanation: Small-scale childcare in the neighborhood encourages new residents to participate more quickly and easily.

206871.explanation: Social rental homes must be fairly distributed among everyone entitled to them.

206872.explanation: We must invest money spent on integration more wisely to help newcomers participate better.

206873.explanation: The VVD wants fewer asylum seekers to come to the Netherlands.

206874.explanation: Brabant and Eindhoven were not involved in the Dutch slave trade. What happened then is terrible, but Eindhoven does not need to feel guilty. However, having conversations with each other is always good.

206875.explanation: Where possible, we should make that possible. However, we are realistic; it is unfeasible to provide every public building with a quiet room.

206876.explanation: It is the responsibility of the business community to properly arrange housing for labor migrants. The municipality can support this.

206877.explanation: Industrial estate De Hurk is the only industrial area for heavy industry in Eindhoven. It is financially unfeasible to relocate all companies. However, the companies must comply with the rules and must invest to become cleaner at an appropriate pace.

206878.explanation: The municipality provides no funding for this. Residents can contact the municipality to receive plants and shrubs.

206879.explanation: Paternalism. Fishing with a fishing license is allowed in the Netherlands and therefore also in Eindhoven.

206880.explanation: The nightlife is part of a big city like Eindhoven. Where possible, entertainment venues are given the freedom to determine their own opening hours.

206881.explanation: Everything is open for discussion in Eindhoven. Safety is paramount and we take all residents of our city into account.

206882.explanation: You borrow from a bank. There are many subsidy schemes that companies wanting to become more sustainable can apply for.

206883.explanation: We must pursue realistic policies, also when it comes to making monumental buildings more sustainable.

206884.explanation: We have too little space in Eindhoven for large solar parks.

206885.explanation: Residents can make use of the Youth Sports Fund and the Learning Money Foundation.

206886.explanation: There must be a good distribution of libraries across our city districts. We encourage that residents have access to books to be able to (learn to) read. 'Bibliotopes' are also pleasant meeting places for residents.

206887.explanation: It is fair to ask for a small contribution.

206888.explanation: We encourage municipal buildings to be used in multiple ways to save costs. We use our resources efficiently.

206889.explanation: Monuments are important for the appearance of our city, but not at any cost.

206890.explanation: A familiar face in the neighborhood gives you a feeling of safety.

206891.explanation: Only in those neighborhoods and streets where it is necessary.

206892.explanation: The VVD does not want visible personal expressions such as religious symbols. Enforcement officers have a neutral role in our society.

206893.explanation: We stand for the people who defend our safety. Enforcement officers must be able to protect themselves.

206894.explanation: In places where necessary, we will install more cameras to combat crime.

206896.explanation: The VVD considers a starters loan a good means to help first-time buyers obtain their first home. It is unrealistic to give away free money. A starters loan should come with an appropriate (low) interest rate.

206897.explanation: We encourage seniors to move on. This frees up large houses for families.

206898.explanation: We build our new homes according to the needs of those seeking housing.

206899.explanation: Eindhoven is growing. We must be open to people from outside. We use waiting lists to determine who is next in line.

206900.explanation: Social rental homes are intended for people with a lower income. Young people generally have not yet built up a high income and therefore naturally qualify for these homes.

206901.explanation: We find it important that Eindhoven is a pleasant city to live in. This also includes sufficient greenery in our city.

206903.explanation: Benefit fraud is antisocial. It disadvantages people who really need it. That is why the VVD wants to tackle fraudsters.

206904.explanation: It is good to help people who are in great need sometimes. But the VVD considers it even more important to prevent these problems.

206907.explanation: The VVD believes that public transport should remain accessible to people with little money.

206908.explanation: There must be enough parking spaces for people with disabilities so they can participate.

206910.explanation: The city center must remain accessible.

206911.explanation: A tram is not financially feasible in Eindhoven.

206912.explanation: Traffic flow is important. However, safety at intersections must be improved.

206913.explanation: The VVD does not think that a maximum speed of 30 km/h should be introduced everywhere. But in residential streets, safety takes precedence over speed.

206914.explanation: This is not a task of the municipality.

206915.explanation: The VVD finds it important that these kinds of things remain feasible. Free parking for informal caregivers is very expensive to monitor.

206856.explanation: D66 makes room for progress. This includes attracting new tech companies, so Eindhoven remains the center of innovation and technology in the future.

206857.explanation: D66 does not see economic growth as a goal in itself, but as a means to invest in a livable city with equal opportunities for everyone. ASML is of great importance for employment, the economy, and gives our region a strategic position in Europe.

206858.explanation: D66 supports sustainable energy and a fair energy transition. So-called small nuclear reactors still take up a lot of space and do not fit into a densely built-up urban area like Eindhoven.

206859.explanation: D66 thinks it is important that elderly people can have a say in municipal policies. A separate senior council is not necessary. D66 prefers good participation for elderly people within existing structures and will strengthen this where needed. This ensures everyone is treated equally.

206860.explanation: D66 wants residents to truly have influence over important issues. A citizens' assembly is a powerful tool to increase support and engagement. The advice from the citizens' assembly must be seriously considered in decision-making. This way, residents recognize themselves in the political choices.

206861.explanation: D66 stands for democratic renewal and good opportunities for citizen participation. A corrective binding referendum could be an example of this.

206862.explanation: A strong city starts with strong neighborhoods. We want places where people meet and feel at home. Therefore, we support residents' initiatives. Neighborhood budgets ensure this can happen in an accessible way without much bureaucracy and paperwork.

206863.explanation: We will not raise the property tax (OZB) unless it really cannot be helped. The OZB should not be increased simply to cover municipal expenses.

206864.explanation: D66 wants companies in Eindhoven to work sustainably and contribute to a clean city. At the same time, Eindhoven is a high-tech and entrepreneurial city where there must be space for people with ideas, courage, and a drive for renewal. This way, we strengthen entrepreneurship, innovation, and sustainability in Eindhoven.

206865.explanation: Eindhoven is already known as a dynamic, enterprising city and region that is of great value to the Netherlands and Europe. We want to cherish and preserve this image. Extra city marketing is not necessary for this.

206866.explanation: Philips is part of Eindhoven's history and culture, and we can be proud of that. Preserving the old headquarters is important to retain this history. D66 will always welcome Philips' return to Eindhoven, but we do not see how the municipality can play an active role in this.

206867.explanation: D66 supports abolishing the dog tax. The dog tax is not used to reinvest in facilities for dogs and is therefore not an efficient tax.

206868.explanation: Local entrepreneurs need more space and freedom. Shopkeepers should be able to choose their own opening hours, including evenings and Sundays.

206869.explanation: D66 supports a ban on fossil fuel advertising, such as ads for holiday flights. D66 has arranged this ban, which will go into effect from 2029. This date was chosen because a new contract period will begin then with the companies responsible for advertising in the city.

206870.explanation: D66 considers good and humane shelter important. Those who come to Eindhoven should have the chance to participate and build their own place. Therefore, D66 supports small-scale shelter spread across the city, with attention to the neighborhood's capacity, so that shelter and integration happen fairly and carefully.

206871.explanation: Refugees allowed to stay in the Netherlands should be able to build a normal life. A permanent home is part of this. This is better for the status holders and for the city. In addition, D66 continues to advocate for more social rental housing so that no one has to wait unnecessarily long for a home.

206872.explanation: D66 believes it is important that everyone who comes to Eindhoven has the opportunity to participate and build their own place. By investing in good integration, newcomers learn the language faster and can better participate in society. This benefits both themselves and the city.

206873.explanation: D66 supports the Dispersion Act. This means every municipality accepts the number of refugees requested of it. This enables Eindhoven to focus on good reception and integration of the newcomers who come here, so that they quickly learn the language and can participate in society.

206874.explanation: Eindhoven has researched its slavery past and holds an annual celebration and remembrance of Keti Koti. D66 wants a slavery monument in Eindhoven. This way, we acknowledge our past and continue building a free, connected city.

206875.explanation: D66 believes that everyone should be free to practice their religion or conviction. Municipal public buildings are neutral and available to everyone. Therefore, D66 supports quiet rooms. These rooms can also be used for prayer but are not dedicated to a single religion.

206876.explanation: In the Netherlands, abuses and exploitation of labor migrants in low-paid jobs are not exceptions. This is unacceptable to D66. Companies are welcome in Eindhoven only if they arrange this properly.

206877.explanation: D66 wants a new, green, and lively neighborhood on the Hurk. Polluting industries have no place on the Hurk. Only if these companies have become sustainable can they return in another location in Eindhoven.

206878.explanation: D66 fully commits to greening the neighborhoods in our city. D66 encourages residents to contribute, for example with a free rain barrel or subsidies for removing pavement from gardens. Neighborhood budgets for greenery fit well with this.

206879.explanation: D66 is against a total ban on recreational fishing. However, D66 wants more attention to animal welfare and environmental care.

206880.explanation: Eindhoven is a vibrant city. The nightlife is not only about relaxation but also about meeting people and talent development. Especially in the evening and at night, musicians and performers take their first steps. Therefore, D66 wants to give the nightlife culture a strong place in the city's cultural life.

206881.explanation: Festivals and parties contribute to a vibrant city. Therefore, it should become easier to organize these events, both during the day and at night. This also gives nightlife culture a strong place in the city's cultural life.

206882.explanation: D66 supports sustainability and a new economy. D66 wants to use the municipal energy company to guide businesses on their sustainability journey. This can be done through a loan fund, where earned interest can be invested in free insulation for private individuals.

206883.explanation: Sustainability must be possible for everyone, even if you live in a monument. D66 supports customized solutions so that monuments can also be insulated.

206884.explanation: D66 supports sustainable energy. However, there is no space in Eindhoven for large-scale energy generation. For generating sustainable energy, we cooperate with the region.

206885.explanation: Sports bring people together, provide enjoyment, and offer everyone the chance to participate. For children, sports and culture must remain accessible. This is how they grow up healthy and safe. Therefore, D66 invests in sports facilities that are accessible to everyone, including people with little money.

206886.explanation: The library is more than a place with books. It is a meeting place, a source of knowledge, and a driver of equal opportunities. Therefore, D66 supports having a library in every neighborhood.

206887.explanation: D66 believes every Eindhoven resident should be able to borrow six books per year for free, without fines. In the long term, we want membership to be completely free for all Eindhoven residents.

206888.explanation: School buildings are often located in the middle of the neighborhood and are therefore ideally suited as accessible, low-threshold locations for associations and social organizations. For example, this provides space for language lessons or band rehearsals.

206889.explanation: D66 considers the monuments in the city very important. We ask private owners to protect them well, and as a city, we must also set a good example ourselves.

206890.explanation: D66 wants more public enforcement officers (BOAs) in the neighborhoods so that residents can have easy and accessible contact. We see that the community police officer is very valuable and want to apply that approach to BOAs as well. This ensures more visibility, trust, and safety in the neighborhood.

206891.explanation: BOAs can play a crucial role in signaling street intimidation and preserving social safety on the streets. For D66, safety goes beyond BOAs and enforcement. It is about a city that shows you belong here, both during the day and at night.

206892.explanation: D66 finds the neutrality of enforcement officers essential. However, religious belief, like gender, skin color, and sexual orientation, is no reason to question the neutrality of people.

206893.explanation: D66 is cautious about arming BOAs. The use of force belongs to the police, who are trained and authorized for this. BOAs are given more and more tasks. If this development continues and BOAs are well trained and educated, carrying a baton may be an option in the future.

206894.explanation: D66 is not against camera surveillance, but it must be necessary and proportional with attention to privacy. D66 prefers more community police officers who are visible and approachable. That creates more contact, trust, and safety without unnecessarily infringing on privacy.

206896.explanation: D66 favors a national homebuyer fund instead of a municipal one. With this fund, we support starters in buying a home. When starters sell the house again, the money returns to the fund. This way we keep the budget available for future starters.

206897.explanation: If elderly people want to move to a smaller home, their family house becomes available for a young family. Therefore, D66 supports helping elderly who want to move, with financial assistance and guidance.

206898.explanation: D66 primarily wants to build lifelong homes, so people can live comfortably even in old age. By building enough suitable homes, including in the higher segment, older people get the chance to move, freeing up other homes for starters and families.

206899.explanation: Young people have a very hard time on the housing market. D66 believes they should have a fair chance to live independently. By reserving 25% of new social rental homes for youth, they regain perspective to build a life. This ensures a fairer housing market.

206900.explanation: Young people have a very hard time on the housing market. D66 believes they should have a fair chance of getting a home. This ensures a fairer and future-oriented housing market.

206901.explanation: D66 chooses to protect green spaces: we do not build in parks and nature. Only if a project truly adds something and is done nature-inclusive, like tiny houses or buildings that enhance existing greenery.

206903.explanation: D66 always works from trust. At this moment, there is no reason to assume large-scale fraud is occurring. If it turns out later that there is widespread fraud, we will reassess.

206904.explanation: D66 is open to freezing and forgiving debts. This helps people move forward. For people receiving benefits, this can provide peace and a fresh start. This only happens when truly necessary, and always carefully and with good guidance.

206907.explanation: D66 believes everyone should be able to move freely through the city, regardless of how much money they have. In addition, D66 strongly invests in public transport and shared mobility. This keeps the city accessible, livable, and attractive.

206908.explanation: Parking takes place as much as possible in garages and parking hubs, making public space more attractive and safer. Some of the regular parking spaces that become available can be retained for people with disabilities.

206910.explanation: Space in Eindhoven is scarce. Therefore, we give pedestrians and cyclists priority, with wide sidewalks, pleasant walking routes, and safe bike paths. We do ensure the city remains accessible by car for residents, entrepreneurs, and those who really need it.

206911.explanation: D66 invests in high-quality public transport; in the future, a metro or tram network would fit the city's growth.

206912.explanation: D66 wants to invest in grade-separated crossings, such as tunnels and viaducts, and reduce speeds to 50 km/h. This makes the ring road safer and improves traffic flow. This contributes to a safe and accessible city.

206913.explanation: Everyone should be able to move safely in Eindhoven. Therefore, the maximum speed in neighborhoods will be lowered to 30 km/h, and streets will be designed accordingly. Research shows that a 30 km/h speed limit is much safer.

206914.explanation: D66 finds it important that people can live independently at home and technology can help with that. Sustainable cooling is becoming increasingly important, but this does not only apply to people over 75. Therefore, D66 does not choose a general subsidy.

206915.explanation: Informal caregivers do important and often heavy work. D66 believes they should experience as few extra barriers as possible. Free parking at the place where they provide care helps with that.

206856.explanation: The SP chooses a people-centered economy focusing on cooperation, solidarity, and participation, also for entrepreneurs. The municipality should not blindly rely on Brainport's growth engine but invest in an economy that benefits all residents.

206857.explanation: Growth of ASML must never be a goal in itself. A good life for all Eindhoven residents is. Housing and basic facilities are not yet even arranged; Eindhoven is not ready for major growth.

206858.explanation: The SP supports clean energy, and that technology is widely available. Eindhoven has not yet fully utilized these possibilities in the city. Nuclear energy is not clean, very expensive, and produces dangerous waste. We can better spend that money on solar and wind energy and clean storage.

206859.explanation: The SP supports participation for every resident of Eindhoven. Now it is mainly consultation without influence on implementation and policy. We want this to change: everyone must be able to participate and have influence. Senior participation can also be part of a broader consultation.

206860.explanation: The SP supports any form of participation and advisory work by Eindhoven residents. The Citizens' Assembly can be one option but must not be the only one. The SP wants real influence from residents in their own city and neighborhood. The municipality must take its residents seriously.

206861.explanation: A binding referendum can be a good way to give residents a decisive voice in policy. Then residents are truly taken seriously.

206862.explanation: Strong neighborhoods are the backbone of a solidary city. A community center, neighborhood center, or bibliotope is a place where neighbors get to know each other. Support for neighborhoods and district development must be structurally arranged from now on.

206863.explanation: Everyone in the city benefits from the property tax (OZB) revenues. If necessary for good social basic facilities, the municipality must have the freedom to determine the OZB rate itself.

206864.explanation: The SP wants companies in Eindhoven to produce as cleanly as possible for the health of employees and local residents. This also includes excluding companies that do not comply.

206865.explanation: Eindhoven is already a very attractive location for companies, resulting in increasing pressure on current residents. Community money should not be spent on this.

206866.explanation: Bringing Philips' headquarters here is not realistic, and the efforts needed for that can be better spent elsewhere. Even without a headquarters, Philips and Eindhoven are connected.

206867.explanation: This is an outdated and obsolete tax.

206868.explanation: If all entrepreneurs could decide this themselves, unfair competition would arise from large companies. We support SMEs because we want to support and protect small entrepreneurs and employees from high work pressure.

206869.explanation: For a healthy and sustainable society, products that are produced in an unhealthy and polluting manner are highly undesirable. Therefore, advertising propaganda for these products should not be allowed.

206870.explanation: The SP supports the dispersed reception of asylum seekers in the city to promote contact with fellow residents and thereby human dignity and integration. This way, asylum seekers can immediately make a positive contribution to our society.

206871.explanation: Status holders currently fall under the municipality's urgency regulation, and the SP wants to keep it that way. The urgency regulation also applies to, among others, victims of domestic violence and single women with small children. Status holders are not the cause of the housing crisis.

206872.explanation: The SP believes integration is more than learning the language and getting to know our culture. Real integration means you may and can work, have a roof over your head, and good access to healthcare and education is arranged. Expats must also integrate; nothing is arranged for that yet.

206873.explanation: Eindhoven already accommodates more people than legally required, and we do this well. We want to maintain that. Most people are already an enrichment to our society.

206874.explanation: Negative effects of colonization and slavery continue to have an impact to this day. Slavery is part of the history of many current Eindhoven residents. This has become clear from research conducted by the municipality. It is important to acknowledge this.

206875.explanation: Where there is a need for this, the organization itself should arrange it. There is already a prayer room in the town hall. It is not up to the municipality to impose this on organizations.

206876.explanation: This of course only applies to companies that employ labor migrants. Expats are also labor migrants. If housing is not properly arranged, the problems fall on the shoulders of residents and the municipality. We want to prevent that. An employer should not also be a landlord.

206877.explanation: Relocating these companies to other places in Eindhoven does not solve the problems but merely shifts them, causing inconvenience to other residents. Real solutions are needed to make polluting companies in all of Eindhoven operate more cleanly.

206878.explanation: A green neighborhood leads to more social connections and a better environment.

206879.explanation: The SP is neutral on this.

206880.explanation: The current policy already allows staying open longer. Making this easier than the current policy would lead to (even) more nuisance and fewer possibilities to address it.

206881.explanation: The SP supports organizing festivals and parties for all Eindhoven residents. However, more consideration must be given to the surroundings and residents when granting permits.

206882.explanation: The municipality of Eindhoven already has possibilities for this; loans are already provided. The SP believes this should remain available for small local businesses and not for large profitable international corporations.

206883.explanation: This must be assessed per monument.

206884.explanation: In those locations we can better build social rental homes. There is an alarming shortage of these in Eindhoven. These houses can have solar panels on their roofs.

206885.explanation: Thanks to the SP, this is now arranged via the current poverty schemes for people earning up to 130% of the minimum wage. The SP wants this to remain so.

206886.explanation: Strong neighborhoods are the backbone of a solidarity city. A community center, district center, or bibliotoop is a place where neighbors get to know each other. Support for neighborhoods, and thus also bibliotops, must be structurally arranged from now on.

206887.explanation: The SP is a strong supporter of this.

206888.explanation: It is a waste if these buildings stand empty in the evenings and weekends while some clubs or social organizations struggle to find affordable space for their activities. The school buildings must then be available at a reduced rate or free of charge.

206889.explanation: To date, we are not aware that insufficient funds are being allocated for this.

206890.explanation: Liveable neighborhoods are also safe neighborhoods. Policy enforcement is a neglected area of the municipality. Permanent municipal enforcement officers can contribute to this. More importantly, the SP believes more neighborhood police officers are needed.

206891.explanation: Municipal enforcement officers are the eyes and ears of the police regarding safety. Safety does not stop in the evenings or weekends.

206892.explanation: There is a constitutional right in the Netherlands to freedom of religion and its expressions. Moreover, this promotes mutual contact and understanding within and between different religious communities.

206893.explanation: Only the police are allowed to use violence; they hold the monopoly on violence in the Netherlands. They are extensively trained for this. Security officers can supervise and observe, but violence intervention belongs with the police.

206894.explanation: There are already many cameras in Eindhoven. They do not prevent crime but increasingly violate citizens' privacy. What does help are preventive measures. These should be applied more.

206896.explanation: The SP wants affordable housing for everyone and also ideally 0% interest, but unfortunately, we are not a bank. If this can be well arranged without placing (too) heavy burdens on the community, we certainly support it.

206897.explanation: The SP supports voluntary housing mobility where possible and wants to financially facilitate it for those who need it.

206898.explanation: Building lifelong homes means residents do not have to incur extra costs as they age. This is not only more efficient but especially cheaper. People of all ages can live here.

206899.explanation: There is a severe shortage of affordable and especially social rental homes for Eindhoven residents. To prevent homelessness and forced relocation outside Eindhoven, they must be given priority.

206900.explanation: There is a significant shortage of social rental homes for young and old in Eindhoven. The SP wants 50% social rental and 40% mid-segment housing realized in all new construction plans.

206901.explanation: The SP is against building in nature, but building on former sports fields (e.g., golf courses) is acceptable to solve the housing shortage.

206903.explanation: Fraud must be tackled and already is. It appears that little deliberate fraud is committed with social assistance. Human errors are sometimes labeled as fraud. We don't want to create a scandal like the childcare benefits scandal in Eindhoven. Trust instead of mistrust should be the starting point.

206904.explanation: People who want to get out of debt and make a new start must be helped, but we want to prevent abuse of this to accumulate more debt.

206907.explanation: This can be arranged through current poverty schemes for people earning up to 130% of the minimum wage. The SP therefore also wants that scheme to remain permanent for that target group (130%).

206908.explanation: Throughout Eindhoven, and especially at all publicly accessible buildings and activities, there must be sufficient disabled parking spaces. The parking spaces must meet the applicable standard and thus be wide enough.

206910.explanation: Of course, the city center must remain accessible for people who depend on a car to get there, and public transport must be greatly improved.

206911.explanation: This costs a lot of money. That money can be better spent on improving current public transportation. That is more efficient and especially cheaper.

206912.explanation: If it benefits safety, health, or the environment, the maximum speed may be lower than 70 km/h.

206913.explanation: For livability and safety, Eindhoven should have a maximum speed limit of 30 km/h in all residential neighborhoods.

206914.explanation: The SP supports providing people with tailored necessary provisions (WMO).

206915.explanation: Informal caregiving should not be hindered by affordability. The existing Caregiver Support Point can provide this.

206856.explanation: With various types of companies in Eindhoven, we are less vulnerable when the economy is weaker. Therefore, Volt wants the municipality of Eindhoven to strongly support a diverse market of (tech) companies in Eindhoven.

206857.explanation: ASML is an indispensable engine for the region. But Volt does not want to make Eindhoven dependent on one sector or one company. We want a broad, innovative economy in which other sectors can also grow and flourish.

206858.explanation: Volt supports nuclear energy, but a 'small' reactor is substantial: as large as three PSV stadiums. Where would that fit in Eindhoven? Volt does want a reactor in the region for smart, constant, and cleaner energy. If the technology is truly safe and compact in the future, Volt is open to a location near the city.

206859.explanation: It is very useful to listen to different target groups. The senior council, as Volt sees it, provides advice on topics they themselves bring forward. It is then up to the city council to weigh that advice.

206860.explanation: Eindhoven residents make the city beautiful, so the municipality really needs to listen to you. A citizens' assembly, where residents are trained to become temporary experts, is the perfect tool for that. Thanks to a motion by Volt Eindhoven in 2025, such a citizens' assembly is finally being conducted in the city.

206861.explanation: Volt wants residents to truly be heard through a citizens' assembly. Careful listening cannot be simply replaced by a yes/no question as that increases division. Volt considers it essential that residents can give input and support early on in the policy process for all new plans for the city.

206862.explanation: The success of the neighborhood budget in Woensel-Zuid shows that the city only becomes more beautiful when you give residents responsibility over it.

206863.explanation: In recent years, property tax rates in Eindhoven and inflation have increased significantly. For the city budget, it is not necessary to raise taxes further than inflation. Therefore, Volt prefers to spare the wallets of residents.

206864.explanation: Volt wants to see as many sustainable businesses as possible in Eindhoven. Volt believes the municipality has the task of being attractive to sustainable companies and helping businesses become more sustainable. According to the law, a municipality cannot simply decide which companies are welcome or not in Eindhoven.

206865.explanation: Eindhoven must be an attractive location, not just appear to be one. A promotion campaign alone is not enough; you also have to be one. Volt believes this can only be achieved through intensive and good collaboration with the local business community. Together, we build a strong economic future.

206866.explanation: What’s the point of that? How much money will that cost? Who will pay for it?

206867.explanation: Pets are just like people residents of the city. Facilities are created for them such as dog parks, and that costs money. It is logical that there is a tax associated with this. However, Volt wants this tax to also be used to improve and expand these facilities.

206868.explanation: Volt believes local entrepreneurs should have the freedom to decide how they manage their shops. Volt has already advocated for this in the past four years.

206869.explanation: Volt wants to go a step further: stop advertisements in public spaces. Advertisements along the road distract traffic. Volt wants a public space that belongs to, is for, and is made by the public. For this, we take Grenoble as an example, where public advertising is banned.

206870.explanation: When we shelter people in Eindhoven, it is especially important that we ensure they can participate in Eindhoven. Integration through participation comes first. By spreading out shelter locations, it is easier to integrate within the neighborhood.

206871.explanation: Those allowed to stay in Eindhoven must be able to build a future. Without a roof over their heads, that is impossible. Besides new construction, we must use existing buildings more intelligently. Volt advocates more housing in current buildings. This way, we quickly offer a home now and make optimal use of the city for everyone starting here.

206872.explanation: Integration is essential, but you cannot just throw money at it and expect it to be done. Volt prefers that the money is spent wisely. Further, Volt believes that employers should pay for the integration of labor migrants.

206873.explanation: Volt believes we should at least comply with the legal obligation. If more is possible, for example in space or support, that is always an option.

206874.explanation: The research into Eindhoven’s slavery past is ongoing. The results and the needs of affected communities must guide the form of apologies or the municipality of Eindhoven’s response.

206875.explanation: Where possible, it is important to provide this. That way, we make Eindhoven an inclusive city for everyone.

206876.explanation: Volt believes new companies should only arrange proper housing if they really intend to employ labor migrants. This prevents nuisance and ensures workers have a pleasant home. Volt does not want to stop company growth unconditionally, as long as the plans for the city and neighborhood are sound.

206877.explanation: Volt wants the municipality to actively enforce environmental laws. Moving polluting industries solves nothing; the nuisance moves along. The core problem is the pollution itself. By truly making industry cleaner, we protect the livability of our region.

206878.explanation: This is called adopteereenstraat.nl and fortunately Eindhoven already knows it. Volt wants to continue this in the coming period.

206879.explanation: Sport fishing is not good for biodiversity or water quality.

206880.explanation: Staggered closing times increase safety. If all bars close simultaneously on Stratumseind, this creates large crowds and disturbances on the street. By staggering closing times, people leave gradually and pressure decreases. This keeps Eindhoven a safe and pleasant place for everyone even at night.

206881.explanation: Volt wants an Eindhoven that buzzes, with festivals and parties for young and old, loud and quiet, large and small scale. This way, we ensure there is (almost) always something to do for everyone in Eindhoven.

206882.explanation: Sustainability measures pay off quickly. Good for the wallet and good for the planet.

206883.explanation: Monuments deserve respect, but sustainability measures should not hinder renovation. However, rules around sustainability should not be a reason for people to hesitate renovating monuments. With creativity, we can become more sustainable without compromising monumental value.

206884.explanation: Volt prefers solar panels on roofs, walls, covered parking areas, and facades. Let’s use the ground for housing, greenery, and recreation.

206885.explanation: Regular sports are good for the whole society; they promote health, community, and sociability. Especially for children’s development, it is essential that they can participate in sports easily. Therefore, Volt wants everyone in Eindhoven to be able to practice sports.

206886.explanation: Volt wants each neighborhood to have its own library. The concept of 'bibliotopes' shows that this is a place to learn, marvel, and connect. For Volt, this doesn’t end there: we want each district to also have its own cultural center.

206887.explanation: Expanding your world should never depend on your income. Volt wants to make the library accessible to everyone because knowledge and reading enjoyment are a right for every resident, regardless of how thick your wallet is.

206888.explanation: Stimulating the association life is good for social cohesion. By using school buildings, scarce space is used efficiently. In addition, it is often cheaper for the associations.

206889.explanation: Volt is explicitly in favor of protecting municipal heritage. The municipality of Eindhoven already invests a lot of money in monuments. Volt wants this money to be spent better.

206890.explanation: We already have neighborhood officers. Recognizability in neighborhoods is great, but that is the job of the police, not the community enforcement officers (boa's).

206891.explanation: In Eindhoven, we do not have enough boa's. It is therefore important to look at where and when deployment is most effective. That can also mean that it is in the evenings or at night.

206892.explanation: By wearing a kippah, turban, or headscarf, boa's are perceived as more approachable by a broad group of residents. Various countries in Europe, such as Sweden and the United Kingdom, have already proven this.

206893.explanation: In the Netherlands, it is strictly regulated who can use force in emergency situations. Volt does not see it as necessary to expand this group in Eindhoven. We have the police for that.

206894.explanation: Volt is not automatically in favor of more camera surveillance. Only when the safety outweighs the violation of privacy do we find placing new cameras justified.

206896.explanation: Starter loans mainly make houses even more expensive. In addition, starters already have a reduced transfer tax.

206897.explanation: A moving allowance will not convince anyone. The costs of moving are not the barrier that stops elderly people from downsizing. Such an allowance would mostly be free money for people who were going to move anyway.

206898.explanation: It is important that we always build as age-proof as possible. That is good for everyone because anyone can suddenly become less mobile.

206899.explanation: Everyone who registers and actively responds must have a chance at a social rental home.

206900.explanation: Young people have not yet had the chance to be on the waiting list for a long time, while they have just as much right to a home.

206901.explanation: Volt considers green spaces important to keep the city livable. Therefore, Volt chooses to densify within the already built-up parts of the city.

206903.explanation: This costs more money than it yields. Do you want your municipal taxes to go up because we have to hire extra civil servants for useless inspections? Volt prefers to spend municipal money on things that also benefit the residents of Eindhoven.

206904.explanation: Buying off debt is cheaper than chasing people with debts and forcing them to pay. In Arnhem, a debt pardon is already in use, which has allowed many people who would normally go through expensive procedures to rebuild their lives.

206907.explanation: In Eindhoven we have the participation contribution that can be used for this. Volt is positive about this.

206908.explanation: Volt would like people with disabilities to be able to park close to their destination, even if it means fewer other parking spaces.

206910.explanation: Car-reduced areas are important to give pedestrians and cyclists as much space as possible in the city center. This makes the city more vibrant, livable, and pleasant.

206911.explanation: Volt wants a fast tram in the future to better connect villages in the region. The province must decide on this plan. Consideration for the tram must already be taken into account when building new roads. Think of high tunnels and wide curves.

206912.explanation: Volt wants the speed on the Rondweg in Eindhoven to be reduced to 50 km/h. This is safer and ensures better traffic flow. Additionally, it contributes to cleaner, healthier air.

206913.explanation: Our children must be able to play safely outside without danger. Therefore, the speed limit in every residential neighborhood must be 30 kilometers per hour. That is safer for everyone and makes neighborhoods more livable.

206914.explanation: Volt wants everyone to live in a healthy and cool environment. Eindhoven is hot in the summer, which unfortunately leads to heat stress. This is dangerous for people with vulnerable health. Volt chooses cooling by planting more trees. One tree cools as much as ten air conditioners.

206915.explanation: Volt believes parking for healthcare should be free. Those who are there for others deserve support, not high parking costs.

206856.explanation: Economic growth must generate social added value.

206857.explanation: Combine growth with housing, accessibility, and livability.

206858.explanation: Does not fit with local sustainable choices.

206859.explanation: Involve elderly people structurally in decision-making.

206862.explanation: Work neighborhood-oriented and give residents control.

206863.explanation: Protect the purchasing power of vulnerable people.

206865.explanation: Not at the expense of social priorities.

206880.explanation: In consultation with residents and with regard for their nighttime rest.

206901.explanation: The housing shortage is so severe that creative solutions are also necessary, which means less green space.

206910.explanation: There are many elderly and disabled people who depend on cars for transport.

206856.explanation: The region is too dependent on ASML. It is wise to add sustainable, future-proof companies, but only once ASML fits within the region's capacity.

206857.explanation: Just like in nature, monocultures are vulnerable. ASML’s growth is still to come, and already there are major housing shortages, insufficient care facilities, etc. Eindhoven must work hard to improve well-being and manage further growth of ASML here.

206858.explanation: Building a nuclear power plant is expensive, takes a long time, and is irresponsible in a densely populated area. Renewable energy is cheaper, cleaner, and faster.

206859.explanation: An advisory council is welcome; it is important that their opinion is also heard. But seniors are a relatively large voting group. Our priority is that the voices of minorities are also loudly heard. Therefore, we remain neutral on this.

206860.explanation: A citizens' assembly can provide broadly supported decisions on complex issues where vested interests are strong, such as in shaping the climate transition.

206861.explanation: Of course, residents should be able to express their opinions on important matters. The referendum will have a role in key decisions, such as major construction projects or municipal reorganizations.

206862.explanation: Residents know well what is needed in their neighborhood. It should be easier to obtain budget for this. By providing a fixed amount, residents can start initiatives themselves that promote social cohesion and combat loneliness.

206863.explanation: In Eindhoven, investments are made in people, animals, and nature. The Real Estate Tax is a means to continue doing this, especially now that the national government is significantly cutting funding to municipalities.

206864.explanation: Polluting companies are not welcome. We already have too many of them. Companies that fit a strong local economy but are not yet fully climate-neutral and clean should be supported during the transition phase. Think, for example, of local bakers.

206865.explanation: There is more than enough employment and promotion for the city. This money can be better spent.

206866.explanation: We do not see added value in this. We prefer to look ahead.

206867.explanation: Dog tax revenues are spent on things unrelated to dogs. Therefore, we will abolish the dog tax, and until that happens, its proceeds will go exclusively to the maintenance of dog facilities.

206868.explanation: Within the limits set by Eindhoven, entrepreneurs are free to decide their opening hours. The Party for the Animals wants opening hours to be part of the permit process, taking into account residents and limiting noise nuisance.

206869.explanation: Absolutely. Advertising encourages overconsumption. Ads for animal products and fossil fuels will disappear first.

206870.explanation: Asylum seekers are welcome in the city. For quick and good integration, spreading them across all neighborhoods is important.

206871.explanation: Without priority, status holders are forced to stay in asylum seeker centers (azc's). This blocks the flow, causing new asylum seekers to end up in inhumane conditions. Status holders have a disadvantage based on registration time and therefore receive priority.

206872.explanation: Many newcomers will come to Eindhoven. That is an enrichment because we can learn from each other. Given the growth, we will need to invest in this so that both old and new Eindhoven residents can benefit.

206873.explanation: Refugees are welcome. The legally mandatory number is the minimum. Eindhoven manages this carefully, with attention to the neighborhood. Considering difficulties elsewhere, we could take a step further.

206874.explanation: The Van Abbemuseum was established with the help of slave labor on tobacco plantations. The community thus benefits from a dark past. Acknowledging this is important for healing relations with descendants of the slaves.

206875.explanation: We see the value of quiet rooms for both religious and non-religious experiences of peace and silence. However, this does not necessarily have to be in every building if alternatives are available nearby.

206876.explanation: Labor migrants often live and work in appalling conditions. Housing is an important condition to prevent this.

206877.explanation: Polluting companies that genuinely add value must become greener and more sustainable. Polluting industries should disappear, not relocate.

206878.explanation: Greening is a joint effort. The 'Adopt a Street' concept remains valid, and the budget can be increased if needed. Besides money, the municipality should also support creating extra public green spaces.

206879.explanation: Of course! The municipality has a hunting ban for land animals, which should be extended to a hunting ban for animals in the water.

206880.explanation: The Party for the Animals considers recreation very important. At the same time, this recreation should not come at the expense of the health of local people and animals. The municipality must therefore set clear frameworks within which nightlife venues can operate freely.

206881.explanation: We are satisfied with the current possibilities. There must be a balance between the benefits for local residents and animals and the partygoers. Animals are very sensitive to noise pollution, so no parties or festivals are allowed in forests and parks.

206882.explanation: We want to reduce emissions as soon as possible; this is a good way. We must also ensure that smaller companies can find the money for sustainability.

206883.explanation: Little can be changed in old buildings designated as monuments to preserve their historical value. The current rules make it very difficult to make them sustainable. The Party for the Animals wants to relax the rules for sustainability.

206884.explanation: Solar panels and greenery will become the norm on roofs, facades, sound barriers, and above parking spaces. Land is too scarce in Eindhoven for solar parks.

206885.explanation: Sports and physical activity have an important place in our society. Your parents' or your own wallet should not be a barrier to being able to participate in sports.

206886.explanation: Every neighborhood should have at least a small-scale library so that, for example, elderly people and children can easily access books and thus promote the joy of reading.

206887.explanation: It is important to us that the price for library membership should not deter anyone.

206888.explanation: In our densifying city, we must use space effectively. If school buildings are suitable for this, we keep costs low for associations and organizations by allowing them to use these buildings outside school hours.

206889.explanation: We must protect municipal monuments well. In our view, funding is currently not the problem.

206890.explanation: The connection with the neighborhood makes the work of the 'neighborhood enforcement officer' more effective.

206891.explanation: Enforcement must be available 24 hours a day. The police and municipal enforcement officers (boa's) should organize this jointly.

206892.explanation: Eindhoven follows the advice of the Human Rights College. Boa's, for example, may wear a headscarf. This allows more diverse people to become boa's and increases identification with the government.

206893.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants fewer harsh measures (repression) and more prevention and youth work. Heavier armament of boa’s does not fit this approach.

206894.explanation: Camera surveillance only offers a false sense of security and does not solve the core of insecurity. The Party for the Animals is cautious about camera surveillance because of privacy infringement. Installing cameras is only allowed temporarily and under strict conditions.

206896.explanation: Municipal starter loans only shift the problem and inflate prices. We want to use the money to build more affordable housing for this group.

206897.explanation: Moving premiums are not an effective instrument. There should be specific housing for the elderly; many people want to move to senior cohousing communities - even without moving premiums - but these do not exist.

206898.explanation: We must build life-course resistant homes suited to the city’s composition.

206899.explanation: Everyone has the right to affordable housing; there should be no distinction based on where people come from.

206900.explanation: The housing shortage among young people is great. But urgency should be leading, not age. A healthy mix in all new construction projects is important.

206901.explanation: Absolutely not. Infill development is still possible. We must build future-proof: nature-inclusive and resistant to weather extremes such as heat waves. This includes a green environment.

206903.explanation: We operate from trust. Investing more in fraud prevention yields little. We prefer to focus on helping people out of social assistance.

206904.explanation: For people on benefits facing severe financial problems, debt restructuring is an important tool.

206907.explanation: Public transport must be accessible to everyone.

206908.explanation: Parking is important for people with physical disabilities. While we want to discourage car ownership and use, this is the exception. Parking policy must take this into account, not only by making parking free but also by ensuring sufficient parking spaces.

206910.explanation: A city center without cars is much more pleasant.

206911.explanation: Eindhoven is growing, and with growth, light rail or trams may be a good investment in the future. For now, we focus on two additional train stations: Tongelre and Acht.

206912.explanation: The ring road will become a 50 km/h zone: better for traffic safety and cleaner air.

206913.explanation: With 30 km/h in residential areas, there are fewer injuries, less exhaust emissions, less noise, and more safety. Additionally, a lower speed limit provides more space for playing. That is why we want more 'living streets' where driving is at a walking pace.

206914.explanation: We gladly invest in natural solutions for heat stress, such as with trees and water.

206915.explanation: Informal care is important, but parking fees are not the way to reward it.

206856.explanation: Extra (financial) efforts for this are unnecessary. The municipality should provide good support or guidance to current businesses if they request it.

206857.explanation: Yes, but within the possibilities and limitations that we as a municipality can offer.

206858.explanation: Although this reactor cannot be located within our city's municipal boundaries. But in consultation with other municipalities and the province, a suitable location must be sought.
