206859.explanation: That is what council members are elected for, and as far as the LPF is concerned, they are always approachable.

206860.explanation: That is what council members are elected for, and as far as the LPF is concerned, they are always approachable.

206861.explanation: That is what council members are elected for, and as far as the LPF is concerned, they are always approachable.

206862.explanation: That is what council members are elected for, and as far as the LPF is concerned, they are always approachable.

206863.explanation: You cannot put it that way. Every municipality has its own financial policy.

206864.explanation: This is formulated too black-and-white. What if a travel agency wants to establish itself that sells flight vacations?

206865.explanation: This is a waste of money because Eindhoven has too little land and development locations where new companies can settle.

206866.explanation: This is like beating a dead horse.

206867.explanation: The collection costs are too high while enforcement is well below standard.

206868.explanation: To keep the city livable, there must be clear agreements about which things may be open during specific time periods.

206869.explanation: This is pure patronizing of free enterprise.

206870.explanation: Then all hell breaks loose and controlling livability and safety in the city will be impossible or poor.

206871.explanation: We as a municipality must not disadvantage our own residents. If we do, it will seriously undermine acceptance of genuine refugees.

206872.explanation: No, not more money, but clear agreements must be made with them about what Dutch society may expect from them.

206873.explanation: Absolutely not! Eindhoven municipality must work together with other municipalities (in the Association of Dutch Municipalities (VNG)) to ensure that the national government stops the inflow of asylum seekers.

206874.explanation: Absolute nonsense. You cannot hold the current generation responsible for something abolished more than 150 years ago.

206875.explanation: If people already work eight hours a day, they still have 16 hours per day to do this at home. Separation of church and state.

206876.explanation: Indeed. The housing shortage is already large and more reskilling will be necessary.

206877.explanation: But let’s first see where in our city and region there is still space to establish these companies.

206878.explanation: The implementation of this must remain in the hands of the municipality.

206879.explanation: No interference in how and where people pursue their hobbies.

206880.explanation: If the municipality wants to monitor livability and safety in the city, it must be examined in consultation whether something is possible or not.

206881.explanation: If by this we mean that there should be less bureaucracy involved, then YES! But livability and safety must not suffer from it.

206882.explanation: That is what banks are for. Municipalities can, if necessary, provide advice.

206883.explanation: Provided we do not cause irreparable damage to the monument.

206884.explanation: Our scarce land is too expensive for that.

206885.explanation: That already happens through additional contributions.

206886.explanation: Provided this is financially feasible. Otherwise they will close after a year due to lack of funds.

206887.explanation: Not for all residents! Operating a library also costs a lot of money. Making it “free” for all city residents means they will contribute in another way. Membership must remain affordable for those on a low income, and we do support that young people up to 21 years old and students while studying should not have to pay a subscription.

206888.explanation: This will not work in practice. Unless we are talking about separate sports halls.

206889.explanation: If the municipal budget allows it.

206890.explanation: Very good because the distance between neighborhood residents and enforcers then becomes smaller.

206891.explanation: That would be ideal. But staff shortages and lack of finances will be the problem.

206892.explanation: A uniform projects authority. No other headgear fits with that.

206893.explanation: A law enforcement officer must be able to defend themselves against violent citizens. Something is better than nothing.

206894.explanation: If we want to maintain safety and livability, more camera surveillance is required.

206896.explanation: That is what banks are for. The municipality can, as has been done previously, establish a certain guarantee or subsidy scheme.

206897.explanation: If we want to have more large houses, that is, under conditions, a good solution.

206898.explanation: Not only downsizing but also remaining affordable for those on a limited income.

206900.explanation: Of course, the target group must be defined.

206901.explanation: If by that we mean the city's area becoming larger, that is acceptable with good policy. But no city parks may be affected.

206903.explanation: If this is necessary, always do it. It is socially irresponsible to allow this fraud to continue.

206904.explanation: Yes, provided it is not their fault. But there are limits and goals. If the person is not a repeat offender, buying them off can get them back in line. Moreover, the municipality can also agree with creditors on reducing the debt.

206907.explanation: Via a special public transport card.

206908.explanation: Because many parking spaces will disappear in Eindhoven, we must take into account extra spaces for people with disabilities.

206910.explanation: Car-reduced means fewer cars. But the city must remain accessible with sufficient parking spaces.

206911.explanation: That would require completely redesigning the infrastructure. Moreover, Eindhoven does not have the space for that.

206912.explanation: Traffic flow at a maximum of 70 km/h is better than lowering it to 50 km/h.

206913.explanation: No, no more anti-car policies in our city.

206914.explanation: No, this is unnecessary discrimination based on age.

206915.explanation: We must ensure that these volunteers can continue their work.

206856.explanation: A good business climate with space for new tech companies ensures innovation, jobs, and economic growth in the region. By focusing on entrepreneurship and reducing regulations, Eindhoven remains attractive for businesses and investments.

206857.explanation: ASML is important for the region and can grow, but without preferential treatment at the expense of local entrepreneurs. Tax money must not be used for forced land sales. If expansion is needed, ASML should buy the land itself and not involve taxpayers.

206858.explanation: A small nuclear reactor provides reliable and affordable energy, unlike unprofitable wind turbines and solar fields. With nuclear energy, the energy supply becomes more stable and long-term costs decrease.

206859.explanation: Older people deserve real influence, not just an extra advisory council. Participation should come through referenda and direct involvement, so everyone is heard equally and policy does not get stuck in yet another consultation group.

206860.explanation: A citizens' assembly only gives influence to a small, randomly selected group and does not put power directly in the hands of all citizens. Therefore, we prefer referenda, so everyone can vote and decisions are truly made by the population.

206861.explanation: Important decisions about Eindhoven affect everyone and therefore should rest with the residents themselves. With a referendum, residents get direct influence on decisions that determine their city and living environment.

206862.explanation: Residents know best what their neighborhood needs, not city hall. By giving neighborhoods a fixed budget, Eindhoven residents get real control and money is spent on what really works locally.

206863.explanation: The property tax must not be a cash cow for the municipality, especially in times when everything is already getting more expensive. By limiting increases, living remains affordable and residents are not confronted with unexpected tax hikes.

206864.explanation: This drives companies away, costs jobs, and harms the local economy. Entrepreneurship must remain free, without ideological barriers or extra regulatory pressure from city hall.

206865.explanation: Eindhoven becomes attractive for companies through low taxes, fewer rules, and fast permits, not by expensive advertising campaigns. The focus must be on removing bureaucracy and giving entrepreneurs room so that companies want to establish themselves here naturally.

206866.explanation: Eindhoven grew because of entrepreneurship and innovation, not rules and bureaucracy. Bringing back the headquarters creates jobs, investments and shows that Eindhoven is once again choosing a strong and free business climate.

206867.explanation: Owning a dog must not be a cash cow for the municipality and is an unfair extra burden for dog owners. That money is better kept by residents rather than disappearing into the municipal treasury.

206868.explanation: Entrepreneurs themselves know best when they can serve their customers. Fewer rules and more freedom lead to more turnover, employment, and a better business climate.

206869.explanation: A ban on 'non-sustainable' advertising is patronizing and infringes on entrepreneurs' freedom. Residents should be able to decide for themselves what they buy and watch, without the municipality pushing them in a certain direction through bans.

206870.explanation: Spreading asylum seeker centers across the city does not solve the problem; it actually causes more nuisance and pressure on neighborhoods. The goal must be to close all asylum seeker centers in Eindhoven.

206871.explanation: Social rental homes are scarce and must primarily be available to our own residents. Priority for status holders puts extra pressure on the housing market and is unfair to starters, families, and elderly from our own city.

206872.explanation: Integration is primarily the responsibility of newcomers themselves, not the taxpayer. Municipal money should go to core tasks for all Eindhoven residents, not to ever more integration projects without clear results.

206873.explanation: More asylum seekers increase pressure on safety, housing and facilities, while Eindhoven is already struggling with this. The municipality must stop bringing in asylum seekers and choose peace, safety, and space for its own residents.

206874.explanation: Eindhoven must look ahead and tackle real problems, instead of participating in symbolic guilt politics.

206875.explanation: Public buildings are for everyone and should remain neutral, without religious facilities that not everyone wants to use. Religion is a personal matter and belongs in churches, mosques, and synagogues, not in city hall or the library.

206876.explanation: Companies are welcome and provide jobs and growth, but labor migration must not automatically increase pressure on housing, facilities, and livability. Therefore, the impact must always be considered, without sealing everything off or throwing the door wide open.

206877.explanation: Forced relocation of companies drives jobs and entrepreneurship out of the city and weakens Eindhoven economically. The municipality must stop ideological environmental policies and focus on employment, growth, and a strong local economy.

206878.explanation: More greenery in the neighborhood makes streets more beautiful, cooler, and nicer to live in. If residents want to get involved themselves, the municipality should support them.

206879.explanation: Sport fishing is an innocent hobby enjoyed by many Eindhoven residents and is part of the use of our public space. A ban is excessive, limits freedom, and needlessly impacts ordinary residents.

206880.explanation: More freedom for hospitality creates a lively city and a stronger local economy. With good enforcement, it remains safe while residents and visitors can enjoy nightlife for longer.

206881.explanation: Fewer rules and faster permits bring more life to the city and give space to local initiatives. Festivals and parties connect Eindhoven residents, strengthen local culture, and help entrepreneurs and associations forward.

206882.explanation: Municipal money belongs to residents and must go to core tasks, not risky loans. Sustainability is a choice for entrepreneurs themselves. The taxpayer must not pay for that.

206883.explanation: Protecting monuments requires care and craftsmanship, not automatically more or fewer rules. (Voluntary) sustainability measures can be applied where appropriate, without damaging the character or burdening owners with costs.

206884.explanation: Large solar parks are expensive and inefficient, put extra pressure on the electricity grid, and occupy valuable land that can be better used for housing and local businesses.

206885.explanation: Sports are important, but contributing to membership fees is not a core task of the municipality. Such schemes always create unfair boundaries, causing people just outside the scheme to be disadvantaged.

206886.explanation: Libraries are valuable in neighborhoods if local residents actually use them. A mandatory library in every neighborhood is unnecessarily expensive and illogical if there is no demand. Tailored solutions per neighborhood are wiser.

206887.explanation: The library is important and must remain accessible, but making it free for everyone costs a lot of money and comes at the expense of other facilities.

206888.explanation: School buildings can play a valuable role for clubs and initiatives, but only when there is local demand. No mandatory opening, but room for local customization, with schools opening only if there is a need and it is practically feasible.

206889.explanation: Municipal monuments tell the story of Eindhoven and give our city character and identity. Investing in them helps preserve these places for future generations instead of allowing them to deteriorate or disappear.

206890.explanation: Safety in the neighborhood requires recognizable faces with real authority; therefore this task primarily lies with the police/district officer. Adding an extra fixed municipal enforcement officer per neighborhood does not solve this and mainly leads to fragmented enforcement.

206891.explanation: Street safety is primarily the responsibility of the police, not municipal enforcement officers. Enforcement officers are useful for supervision and order but should not replace the police by being deployed 24/7 everywhere.

206892.explanation: Enforcement officers should have one clear appearance, so it is immediately clear to everyone that they act on behalf of the government. Religious symbols on uniforms cause division and do not belong in neutral street enforcement.

206893.explanation: Enforcement officers are there for supervision and order, not to take over police duties. Increased safety is achieved, among other things, by having more police on the street, not by arming municipal enforcement officers.

206894.explanation: Street safety is important, but more cameras are not always the solution. Camera surveillance can be useful at specific problem locations but should not lead to permanent surveillance and infringement of privacy.

206896.explanation: A starter’s loan at 0 percent interest drives housing prices up further and mainly benefits sellers, not starters. The solution lies in more affordable housing and lower costs, not in new loans that increase debt pressure.

206897.explanation: Moving is a personal choice and not a municipal task. That money can be better used to reduce municipal taxes for everyone.

206898.explanation: Starters and young families have been at the back of the housing market for years and urgently need affordable homes now. Strict age quotas reduce building freedom and only increase the housing shortage.

206899.explanation: Eindhoven residents often wait years for an affordable home and therefore deserve priority. This keeps living in their own city feasible for people who have built their lives here.

206900.explanation: Young people have been on waiting lists for years and are being pushed out of their own city. Reserving a fixed part of social rental housing for young people gives them a fair chance again.

206901.explanation: Eindhoven residents have been waiting too long for affordable housing. Building should take priority, with attention for smart integration of green spaces in new neighborhoods. Without extra space for housing, starters and families remain sidelined while the housing crisis grows.

206903.explanation: Money for social assistance is intended for those who truly need it, not for abuse. By tackling fraud more strictly, the system remains fair and more remains available for those who genuinely deserve support.

206904.explanation: Buying off and forgiving debts does not solve the root causes and rewards irresponsible choices, while the cost falls on the hard-working Eindhoven resident. Help should focus on guidance and perspective, not on wiping out debts with taxpayers’ money.

206907.explanation: Free public transport sounds appealing, but the cost ultimately falls on the hard-working Eindhoven resident through higher taxes. Make public transport affordable for everyone by lowering prices, instead of selective free schemes.

206908.explanation: For many people (including those with disabilities), a car is not a luxury but a necessity. More parking spaces ensure they can move independently and without extra obstacles in the city.

206910.explanation: A car-restricted city center makes Eindhoven less accessible for residents and entrepreneurs who depend on their car or van. Shops and hospitality lose customers, and the city becomes unnecessarily expensive and impractical for people who just want to live, work, and shop here.

206911.explanation: Building a tram causes years of disruption in the city and costs a lot of money. That money can be better spent on better and affordable public transport.

206912.explanation: The planned reduction to 50 km/h is an unnecessary punishment for motorists and mainly causes frustration. It is better to invest in improved intersections and smart solutions to allow traffic to flow safely and smoothly.

206913.explanation: A general 30 km/h limit makes neighborhoods unnecessarily hard to reach and causes mainly extra frustration. Traffic safety is achieved with tailored solutions and smart design, not with an ideological speed limit across the entire city.

206914.explanation: Air conditioners can help with heat, but subsidies are not the right approach. Municipal money should not go to individual purchases but to lower costs and solutions that benefit everyone.

206915.explanation: Informal caregivers do indispensable work for family and acquaintances and deserve support instead of extra costs. Free parking removes barriers and keeps care close and humane.

206856.explanation: Economic growth is important for employment and opportunities for Eindhoven residents. New companies are welcome if they contribute to fair jobs, good working conditions, and investment in education, so prosperity is broadly shared in the city.

206857.explanation: Cooperation with large employers can contribute to jobs and innovation in the region. For DENK, it is essential that growth goes hand in hand with good housing, fair wages, and opportunities for local residents and youth.

206858.explanation: DENK chooses a healthy and safe living environment. We focus on energy saving, insulation, solar panels, and clean mobility, because these solutions are affordable, locally feasible, and immediately beneficial to residents.

206859.explanation: We believe that older people should actively participate in policy. With a senior council, neighborhood service points, and strong support for informal caregivers, we ensure their experiences and ideas are directly included in municipal decisions.

206860.explanation: We believe that residents should actively contribute to policy. Through citizen assemblies, resident panels, and experiential experts, we ensure decisions are fairer and better aligned with what is happening in all neighborhoods of Eindhoven.

206861.explanation: We prefer other forms of participation, such as resident panels, neighborhood meetings, and actively involving experiential experts. This way, residents can not only express their opinions but also genuinely contribute and discuss content and considerations before decisions are made.

206862.explanation: We believe that residents should be able to co-decide about their neighborhood. With fixed neighborhood budgets, we give residents the chance to choose for greenery, playgrounds, safety, and meeting places, making every neighborhood fairer and more livable.

206863.explanation: We believe that low-income residents should maintain financial room. Therefore, property tax (OZB) should not rise faster than prices in the Netherlands. This prevents housing and living from becoming unaffordable for people with the smallest budgets.

206864.explanation: We consider sustainability important, but also that everyone can participate in the economy. We want to encourage and support companies to become greener, rather than exclude them upfront, so jobs and opportunities for Eindhoven residents continue to grow.

206865.explanation: Public money should first go to jobs, education, and support for local and social entrepreneurs. Promotion can help, but our priority lies with strong neighborhoods and fair opportunities for Eindhoven residents, so economic growth directly benefits the city.

206866.explanation: We prioritize local entrepreneurs, jobs, and education for Eindhoven residents. Bringing back headquarters is less important than ensuring economic growth directly contributes to strong neighborhoods and fair opportunities in the city.

206867.explanation: We believe that charges for residents must be fair. By abolishing the dog tax, low-income residents keep more financial room. This ensures having a pet does not become unnecessarily expensive.

206868.explanation: We believe that local entrepreneurs should have the freedom to decide what works best for their business and neighborhood. This allows them to better respond to their customers and grow their business without unnecessary municipal regulations.

206869.explanation: Public spaces must contribute to a healthy and sustainable living environment. Advertising for non-sustainable products does not fit this. We encourage more conscious choices and want the municipality to actively contribute to a healthier future for everyone.

206870.explanation: We believe that shelter must be humane and small-scale. By distributing asylum seekers across the city, we prevent nuisance and ensure better guidance, more contact with local residents, and faster opportunities for integration into society.

206871.explanation: Status holders are residents of Eindhoven entitled to a dignified start. Quick housing promotes integration, work, and education, and prevents long-term sheltering. At the same time, we focus on more social housing so solidarity does not come at the expense of others.

206872.explanation: We believe newcomers should be able to participate in the city quickly. With extra investments in language, guidance, work, and neighborhood activities, we ensure they become independent faster and are part of their district and Eindhoven society.

206873.explanation: Eindhoven takes responsibility for humane shelter. Additional shelter is possible when there is sufficient support, facilities, and guidance in the neighborhoods so that both newcomers and current residents are well supported.

206874.explanation: We believe recognition is important for justice and connection. With apologies and attention to the colonial and slavery past, we show that we learn from history and work together on respect and equal opportunities for everyone.

206875.explanation: We believe public buildings should provide space for everyone. Quiet rooms are places for reflection and prayer, but also for people who need rest. This ensures inclusion, respect, and well-being in our city.

206876.explanation: We believe growth must be fair and responsible. New companies are welcome, but only if housing for labor migrants is well arranged. This prevents pressure on neighborhoods and ensures dignified living for everyone.

206877.explanation: We believe a healthy living environment is important. Pollution must be tackled, but we want to help companies work cleaner and more sustainably first. Relocation can be an option if improvement is not possible and neighborhoods continue to suffer.

206878.explanation: We believe residents should be able to make their neighborhood greener and healthier. With funds for shrubs and plants, we help residents beautify their district together, improve air quality, and create more places to meet each other.

206879.explanation: We value a healthy living environment, but also space for recreation. Our focus is on protecting nature and water quality and on clear rules so that people and nature remain in balance without immediately banning everything.

206880.explanation: We believe a lively city is important, but also peace and safety in neighborhoods. We focus on good agreements with entrepreneurs, residents, and enforcement so that going out is possible without increased nuisance and insecurity.

206881.explanation: We believe culture and meeting each other bring people together. By making festivals and celebrations easier, we create room for neighborhood initiatives and diverse communities. This strengthens connection, creativity, and a vibrant city for everyone.

206882.explanation: We believe sustainability must be affordable for entrepreneurs. With municipal loans, we help businesses become greener, reduce costs, and contribute to a healthy living environment without sacrificing jobs and the local economy.

206883.explanation: We believe monuments should also contribute to a healthy and sustainable city. With more flexible rules, we enable sustainability measures such as insulation and energy savings without losing the building's character.

206884.explanation: Land in Eindhoven is scarce and needed for housing, greenery, and livable neighborhoods. Sustainability is better achieved through solar panels on roofs and public buildings, generating clean energy without sacrificing space for residents.

206885.explanation: We believe sports should be accessible to everyone. With municipal support for memberships, we help people with little money participate, stay healthy, and build new contacts in their neighborhood and club.

206886.explanation: We believe knowledge and meeting places should be close to everyone. With a library in every neighborhood, residents gain better access to reading, learning, and digital assistance, creating a safe place for meeting, development, and support.

206887.explanation: We believe access to reading, learning, and digital help is a right for everyone. By making the library free, we give low-income people, youth, and families more opportunities to develop and participate in society.

206888.explanation: We believe school buildings should also be places for the neighborhood outside school hours. By opening them up to clubs and social organizations, we strengthen meetings, sports, culture, and equal opportunities in all neighborhoods.

206889.explanation: We believe Eindhoven's heritage is important for identity and connection. With extra funding for protection and maintenance, we ensure monuments are preserved and remain accessible for residents and activities in the neighborhood.

206890.explanation: We believe safety starts with trust in the neighborhood. With dedicated community enforcement officers (boa’s) per district, officers know the residents and their problems better. They can assist faster, work preventively, and create a safer and more pleasant living environment.

206891.explanation: We believe safety is important at all times. With 24/7 deployment of community enforcement officers, we ensure visibility and quick assistance, also on weekdays. This increases the sense of safety in neighborhoods and enables faster tackling of nuisance and problems.

206892.explanation: We believe community enforcement officers should remain professional and recognizable by their uniforms. Wearing a headscarf, kippah, or turban does not change this but shows that enforcement reflects Eindhoven's diversity and makes everyone feel welcome.

206893.explanation: Safety begins with trust and proximity. Community enforcement officers must be visible, approachable, and able to de-escalate. Through collaboration with residents and neighborhood-oriented prevention, we increase safety without further hardening.

206894.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to safety in places with structural nuisance. This must always be done with clear privacy rules and in combination with neighborhood enforcement officers and social prevention, so that supervision remains humane.

206896.explanation: We believe young people and starters should have more opportunities to get their own home. With an interest-free starter loan, we help people with little savings buy a house and ensure housing remains accessible for Eindhoven residents.

206897.explanation: We believe elderly people should be supported when they move to smaller housing. With a relocation bonus, we help them move comfortably and free up larger homes for families and starters, making housing availability fairer and faster in the city.

206898.explanation: Of all new homes to be built in Eindhoven, at least one third should be suitable for people aged 50 and over.

206899.explanation: We believe Eindhoven residents—such as youth, starters, families, and elderly—should have a fair chance at housing in their own city. With local priority, we ensure people who live, work, or provide care here are not left behind.

206900.explanation: We believe new construction should be fairly distributed according to the 40-40-20 rule: many affordable homes, space for middle incomes, and a smaller portion for the higher segment. Within this distribution, we allocate fixed places for youth, starters, and elderly.

206901.explanation: We believe expansion may be necessary for affordable housing. Green areas that do not need to disappear will be preserved. We build carefully and ensure new parks and green buffers so neighborhoods remain healthy and livable for everyone.

206903.explanation: We believe extra funding for fraud detection should not be the main focus. We do support fair and transparent inspections without discrimination. Our priority lies with guidance, trust, and help with work and debts to prevent problems.

206904.explanation: We believe people with debts should get perspective for a new start. Support from the municipality can also help with smaller debts to create financial peace, providing guidance towards a stable income and better money management.

206907.explanation: We believe people with little money should be able to move freely. Free public transport helps reach work, school, care, and family. This prevents poverty leading to isolation and provides equal opportunities for everyone in the city.

206908.explanation: We believe accessibility should be broader than just more parking spaces. Our focus is on inclusive public spaces, good public transport, and safe sidewalks and buildings so people with disabilities can move independently throughout the city.

206910.explanation: We want a livable and accessible city center. Public transport and cycling should be promoted, but car use must not suffer due to policy. Many residents depend on cars for work, care, and accessibility.

206911.explanation: Good public transport is important, but large and costly projects must not come at the expense of affordability. We prioritize fast, accessible connections in all neighborhoods, such as neighborhood buses and improved existing transit lines.

206912.explanation: The maximum speed on Eindhoven's ring road must remain 70 km/h.

206913.explanation: Traffic safety is paramount, especially around schools and busy residential streets. Instead of a general measure, we choose tailor-made solutions to ensure accessibility for residents, entrepreneurs, and emergency services.

206914.explanation: We believe elderly people should be protected against heat and health risks. Subsidies for cooling can be provided under clear conditions, such as high vulnerability or poorly insulated homes, ensuring help reaches those who need it most.

206915.explanation: People who care for family members or acquaintances must be able to park for free at that location in Eindhoven.

206856.explanation: Eindhoven is already known as a highly innovative city with many tech startups. We want to maintain this and attract new companies to come innovate here.

206857.explanation: The growth of ASML must not come at the expense of the city's recognizable character. English is becoming the norm. Residents feel the pressure on the housing market due to homebuying. We must ensure that every Eindhoven resident can benefit from growth.

206858.explanation: Nuclear energy makes an indispensable contribution to a continuous and CO2-free energy supply. The municipality must take firm control of the energy transition in the interest of residents and entrepreneurs.

206859.explanation: We find it unnecessary to create an extra advisory body for this group. About half of eligible voters are over 50, and this group most often turns out to vote.

206860.explanation: We do not consider citizens' assemblies a productive form of citizen participation. It is a very high threshold for citizens to participate. Moreover, advice not adopted can lead to frustration and false hope among residents. We therefore advocate for a referendum.

206861.explanation: A referendum forces politicians to stand behind their decisions and clearly present them to the population. That is why we want to hold proactive referendums for every major municipal decision so citizens can participate in time on important topics.

206862.explanation: Neighborhood residents know the needs of their area better than civil servants. We believe in shared responsibility: the municipality must trust its residents and give them space to improve the city together. This way we ensure neighborhoods retain their character.

206863.explanation: We stand firmly for the security of residents' livelihoods. We do not want to impose unnecessary obstacles, so the municipality must ensure that property tax (OZB) does not rise faster than inflation. This keeps living in Eindhoven feasible and affordable.

206864.explanation: We oppose extra suffocating regulations for businesses. Entrepreneurs must be able to operate without strict rules. NSC Eindhoven is not against sustainability policies per se, but wants to prevent entrepreneurs from falling between the cracks because of them.

206865.explanation: In our view, this is not necessary. Eindhoven is already known as a highly innovative city with many startups and SMEs.

206868.explanation: Where possible, entrepreneurs should be able to determine their opening hours. This gives businesses as much freedom as possible to operate.

206869.explanation: We are critical of patronizing measures. People are responsible enough to handle advertisements well. Moreover, the label "sustainable" is very broad and could lead to banning anything not fully organic from advertising.

206871.explanation: Everyone in an untenable situation retains the right to urgency status. However, the housing shortage in the city forces us to reduce the pressure.

206872.explanation: To keep Eindhoven recognizable, we must strongly focus on integrating newcomers. An integral approach together with Eindhoven residents is also needed. We want to set up local networks so newcomers can integrate informally in their neighborhoods.

206875.explanation: We attach great importance to the separation of religion and government. That is why we also keep religious expressions separate from government buildings.

206883.explanation: We stand for accessible communication from the municipality. The rules should therefore be as simple as possible, without compromising quality.

206888.explanation: Certainly. We want to give associations and social organizations as much space as possible in Eindhoven. So wherever possible, it should be allowed.

206889.explanation: We have a warm heart for municipal monuments, but currently we do not see the need to allocate extra funds for their protection. We want to handle taxpayers' money responsibly and do not see this as efficient.

As far as we are concerned, the role of the community officer should grow and they must build a network of trust in the neighborhood. The enforcement officers can support this, but the central point remains the community officer.

206891.explanation: Certainly. Too many Eindhoven residents, including women and children, still feel unsafe outdoors, and therefore it is important to provide safety throughout the city.

206892.explanation: The strength of our police and enforcement is that they can act as neutral outsiders in sometimes emotionally charged situations. We therefore want to strictly maintain this neutrality by not allowing staff to express religious symbols.

206893.explanation: Enforcement officers support the work of the police. Police officers should act when the situation requires it.

We will also accelerate housing construction by removing unnecessary regulations. In addition, we maintain the affordability standard to ensure cheaper homes are built.

206897.explanation: Currently, the elderly already have priority for social rental homes in the senior segment. Furthermore, seniors are generally relatively wealthy, so it would be unfair to taxpayers to offer every senior a relocation allowance.

206898.explanation: We are committed to keeping the city accessible, including housing. However, seniors already have priority for social rental homes in the senior segment. Reserving a third of all new homes for seniors is also a very large portion.

206899.explanation: As NSC Eindhoven, this is one of our core points. Eindhoven’s growth puts enormous pressure on the housing market. We want to ensure that people born and raised in Eindhoven can continue to live here and are not displaced. Eindhoven must be a city that is also a home.

206900.explanation: To give young people a fair chance at their own home, NSC Eindhoven strongly advocates this. Young people currently suffer the most from the pressure on social rental housing. Too few social rental homes are being built and the waiting time is often at least eight years.

206901.explanation: Currently, the housing shortage is very large and we must make fair choices. Building homes is the top priority. However, we prefer to build new homes with green spaces so that people do not only have a view of concrete but can also get some fresh air.

206903.explanation: As NSC, we know better than anyone what a tough approach can do. We want to combat fraud, but focusing heavily on it will lead to a second benefits scandal. Moreover, fraud in welfare benefits is relatively rare and not productive to focus on.

206904.explanation: The Netherlands has a debt industry that is not aimed at helping people in debt but at collecting money. Therefore, as municipalities, we do not want to forgive debts but to take them over and work together with the debtor towards a humane solution for their situation.

206907.explanation: Making public transport in Eindhoven free is a priority for NSC Eindhoven. Especially for people with little money, the bus is an important part of their life in Eindhoven. Ideally, we want to establish the Eindhoven Transport Company (EVB), which ensures affordable and quality public transport.

206908.explanation: NSC Eindhoven stands for a city where no one is left behind. That is why we are committed to making the city as accessible as possible, especially for people with disabilities.

206910.explanation: We believe the city center must remain accessible, also for motorists. We therefore oppose the municipality’s traffic circularity plan that aims to exclude cars from the city as much as possible.

206911.explanation: We are open to tram connections in Eindhoven. However, our priority is to improve public transport in terms of quality and affordability. Therefore, we focus on establishing the Eindhoven Transport Company (EVB). This starts with bus lines but can expand to tram lines.

206912.explanation: NSC Eindhoven wants to give motorists enough space to travel. This way, we keep the city accessible and functional. Lowering the speed will lead to unnecessary obstacles. We therefore oppose the municipality’s traffic circularity plan that aims to remove cars from the city.

206913.explanation: Where this is not yet the case, NSC Eindhoven is open to reducing the maximum speed to 30 km/h. This ensures our neighborhoods remain safe.

206856.explanation: PRO Eindhoven says yes, because innovative tech companies contribute to employment and economic development in the Brainport Eindhoven region. This growth must be balanced with livability, accessibility, and residents' interests, so development strengthens the city as a whole.

206857.explanation: In line with our program, PRO Eindhoven chooses to collaborate with high-tech companies, provided growth is accompanied by investments in affordable housing, mobility, and facilities for residents.

206858.explanation: PRO Eindhoven focuses on development through sustainable solutions that can be implemented locally. Nuclear energy does not fit municipal responsibilities and does not contribute to a rapid improvement of livability.

206861.explanation: PRO Eindhoven supports referendums, where residents are actively involved in advance and the referendum serves as the democratic conclusion of an open and careful participation process.

206863.explanation: Fair governance means predictable expenses for residents and entrepreneurs, as PRO Eindhoven emphasizes in its program.

206864.explanation: PRO Eindhoven sets sustainability as the norm for new businesses. Companies wishing to establish themselves must operate sustainably or demonstrably be in transition towards sustainability, with clear agreements and support from the municipality.

206865.explanation: A city becomes attractive through good governance, not campaigns. Effective policy and a reliable government that thinks along with businesses are more important than extra money for promotion.

206866.explanation: PRO Eindhoven chooses a level playing field for all companies and no symbolic policies without direct added value for residents.

206867.explanation: This is an outdated charge without a direct return; the proceeds disappear into general funds while dog owners face additional administrative burdens. PRO Eindhoven opts for less bureaucracy and fair rules that work for residents and therefore wants to abolish this tax.

206868.explanation: Entrepreneurs and neighborhoods know what works themselves. This aligns with neighborhood-oriented and practical policy.

206869.explanation: PRO Eindhoven disagrees because a general advertising ban on non-sustainable products is hard to implement and enforce, and sustainability is better promoted with clear, targeted measures that work in practice.

206870.explanation: PRO Eindhoven's election program explicitly states that shelter must be fairly distributed across neighborhoods, without overburdening vulnerable areas.

206871.explanation: PRO Eindhoven chooses justice and equal opportunities in housing allocation.

206872.explanation: According to the program, investing in language, work, and participation is essential for strong neighborhoods and social cohesion.

206873.explanation: The program focuses on support, capacity, and livability; the legal task is the starting point.

206874.explanation: Eindhoven should primarily focus on connection and looking ahead. We consider recognition and education about the colonial and slavery past important, but choose a careful local approach that connects and contributes to living together, instead of formal apologies.

206875.explanation: PRO Eindhoven opts for inclusion and meeting through customized and practical solutions but rejects a general obligation for quiet rooms in all public buildings.

206876.explanation: PRO Eindhoven is positive about companies that contribute to work and development but does not see housing for labor migrants as a condition that the municipality should enforce beforehand.

206877.explanation: PRO Eindhoven disagrees because not every polluting company should automatically have to relocate. We choose customized solutions: reducing nuisance and pollution through clear rules, supervision, and sustainability, so livability and sustainability improve without unnecessary relocations.

206878.explanation: Investing in neighborhoods and citizen initiatives is a spearhead of PRO Eindhoven's program.

206879.explanation: PRO Eindhoven chooses a balance between recreation, animal welfare, and clear rules.

206880.explanation: PRO Eindhoven agrees because a vibrant nightlife contributes to a lively city and economic development. Extending opening hours can be considered, provided this is done carefully with clear agreements on safety, supervision, and livability and in good consultation with residents.

206881.explanation: Culture and events strengthen social cohesion and a vibrant city, as mentioned in the program.

206882.explanation: PRO Eindhoven chooses sustainable development by actively supporting companies in sustainability efforts, promoting progress rather than just control.

206883.explanation: PRO Eindhoven wants to make monuments more energy-efficient and future-proof while preserving their historical value and therefore opts for more flexible and clearer rules that allow customization.

206884.explanation: PRO Eindhoven prefers to use scarce land for housing and livable neighborhoods and prioritizes solar energy on roofs and existing buildings first.

206885.explanation: Equal opportunities for children and adults through sports are an explicit program point.

206886.explanation: PRO Eindhoven wants every neighborhood to have access to library services but opts for an affordable mix of online offerings and neighborhood-oriented physical facilities.

206887.explanation: PRO Eindhoven agrees because access to knowledge, information, and development should be easily accessible to everyone. The library is already free for children; by arranging this for all residents, we strengthen equal opportunities, education, and participation in society.

206888.explanation: By opening school buildings outside school hours in a controlled and safe manner with clear agreements, facilities can be better utilized and neighborhoods and associations strengthened.

206889.explanation: PRO Eindhoven agrees because protecting municipal monuments contributes to a recognizable, livable, and future-proof city.

206890.explanation: Neighborhood-oriented work and familiar faces increase safety and trust.

206891.explanation: Safety should be nearby and available, as the program indicates.

206892.explanation: This is already allowed in Eindhoven; PRO Eindhoven believes enforcement officers (boa’s) should work neutrally and professionally, and religious expressions can be present as long as safety, recognizability, and equal treatment are guaranteed.

206893.explanation: PRO Eindhoven believes enforcement officers should be able to do their work safely but sees the baton only as an option when it demonstrably contributes to safety, with proper training, clear rules of engagement, and supervision.

206894.explanation: PRO Eindhoven believes camera surveillance can be permanently used in busy and high-risk places such as stations and nightlife areas but not as a general standard measure throughout the city.

206896.explanation: Supporting first-time homebuyers fits with fair housing, provided it is financially responsible.

206897.explanation: PRO Eindhoven considers a moving allowance expensive and ineffective. The effect is limited and the administrative burden high. Therefore, PRO Eindhoven prefers structural investments in senior housing and better housing market flow.

206898.explanation: The city must be prepared for aging and suitable housing.

206899.explanation: PRO Eindhoven finds it fair that home seekers from Eindhoven get priority in allocating social rental homes as much as possible, within legal possibilities and maintaining a fair and transparent system.

206900.explanation: Equal opportunities for youth are an explicit spearhead in the program.

206901.explanation: PRO Eindhoven disagrees because housing development is necessary but should not come at the expense of the green environment. We choose smart densification and careful use of space, where preservation of green and livability weigh heavily in spatial decisions.

206903.explanation: PRO Eindhoven believes misuse of facilities must be addressed to maintain support for social security, where control always goes hand in hand with support and the human dimension.

206904.explanation: PRO Eindhoven agrees because buying up and forgiving debts can offer people with serious financial problems a perspective, where help is provided first and control afterwards, aiming for a debt-free future.

206907.explanation: Mobility is necessary to participate in society.

206908.explanation: PRO Eindhoven agrees because sufficient parking spaces for people with disabilities are necessary for accessible and independent participation in daily life.

206910.explanation: PRO Eindhoven supports a car-reduced city center where this improves livability and safety but believes the city center must remain easily accessible for residents, entrepreneurs, emergency services, and people with disabilities.

206911.explanation: PRO Eindhoven chooses smart, affordable mobility solutions instead of costly and risky projects.

206912.explanation: This contributes to traffic flow, traffic safety, and livability.

206913.explanation: PRO Eindhoven supports a maximum speed of 30 km/h in residential neighborhoods where this demonstrably contributes to traffic safety and livability but believes this should be customized per neighborhood and street.

206914.explanation: PRO Eindhoven disagrees because a general air conditioning subsidy is not appropriate; we choose customized and targeted, preferably sustainable support for elderly who really need it.

206915.explanation: PRO Eindhoven believes informal caregivers deserve practical support so they can perform their care tasks without extra barriers, such as parking fees.

206856.explanation: Our region has a unique position in the world with ASML; strengthening that position by attracting new tech companies is sensible for our generation but also for the coming generation(s).

206857.explanation: We must not miss the opportunities offered by the development of ASML. It is undisputedly number one in the world, but we must continue to invest to maintain or even expand that position.

206858.explanation: For the ChristianUnion, nuclear energy is part of the energy mix and should be considered more broadly than just the region; this must be national policy. In our view, a few large nuclear power plants is a better idea than small and regional ones.

206859.explanation: In our opinion, the elderly are sufficiently represented in the Eindhoven city council.

206860.explanation: We agree with this statement but with some reservations; naturally, the council must listen to what is happening in society, but that does not necessarily have to be strictly organized.

206861.explanation: The council has the mandate from the people of Eindhoven and with that the responsibility to govern the city and make broad considerations. A referendum can be driven too easily purely by emotion.

206862.explanation: With the caveat that 'annually' and a 'fixed amount' are formulated far too rigidly for our liking. Neighborhoods, for example organized in livability teams, are in close contact with municipal area coordinators. Good initiatives must be discussed and weighed there.

206863.explanation: That would be nice, but we face major challenges and those cost money. The ChristianUnion can imagine that this might lead to slightly higher charges for citizens, who for the most part also benefit from the strength of the region. We support generous debt forgiveness policies for those with minimum incomes.

206864.explanation: We find this statement far too easy. When do we consider a company to operate sustainably? All companies must comply with the permits granted, and the municipality must strictly enforce this, even shutting down companies if necessary.

206865.explanation: We do not think this is necessary now. Eindhoven is sufficiently 'on the map.' Companies really know how to find us.

206866.explanation: Eindhoven should never have let it get this far that the headquarters left the city, but trying to get it back now seems a bit pointless to us.

206867.explanation: In the long term, this will certainly happen, as is the national trend, but the loss of income will then have to be covered by people who do not have a dog. Compared to the total costs of owning a dog, the dog tax is just a small amount.

206868.explanation: This is also a much too broad statement; local entrepreneurs can determine their own opening and closing times but are often forced to be open by large national chains. We do see the dangers of a 24-hour economy and the value of enough rest in society.

206869.explanation: When is a product sustainable? There are many products that are not allowed to be advertised (alcohol/tobacco). That policy should be regularly reviewed and adjusted where necessary.

206870.explanation: The ChristianUnion feels responsible to provide decent shelter and prospects for asylum seekers; but not unlimited. This is a national theme where we must work locally. In our view, asylum seekers must contribute much faster to society (education/work/volunteering).

206871.explanation: The housing market is complex and tight. We must deal with it wisely, and customized solutions fit better than generic measures. Above all, a lot – really a lot – of building must take place.

206872.explanation: 'More money' is rarely a solution. We must do the right things and integration is definitely part of that. We especially want newcomers to learn the language quickly (English would be acceptable to us) and be able to contribute to society through (volunteer) work.

206873.explanation: Even if we meet the legal requirements, there will still be more than enough problems to solve in the housing market.

206874.explanation: This is not a theme for Eindhoven. This is a national theme.

206875.explanation: Silence is a rare commodity these days. Quiet spaces would be very welcome.

206876.explanation: Companies have a responsibility for their employees.

206877.explanation: But we want to go much further: the entire Hurk must be rezoned to residential area. All business activity must disappear there. An additional advantage is that the houseboats can stay in the Beatrix Canal.

206878.explanation: All good initiatives from neighborhoods must be seriously considered and implemented; including planting/plantings/green spaces.

206879.explanation: For many people fishing is a moment of peace and relaxation. We certainly want that to continue.

206880.explanation: Existing arrangements offer more than enough possibilities in our opinion.

206881.explanation: Existing arrangements offer more than enough possibilities in our opinion.

206882.explanation: Companies can simply present investment plans to banks or shareholders. We see no role for the municipality here.

206883.explanation: Monuments must be preserved as much as possible 'as they are' and that requires very careful handling of modifications.

206884.explanation: That space simply does not exist. However, we do have many (hundreds of) thousands of square meters of roofs where nothing is yet installed.

206885.explanation: Everyone should be able to play sports and money should not be a limitation for that.

206886.explanation: Eindhoven should never have cut the library service. At least as many as there were before should be restored.

206887.explanation: Many people can easily afford a subscription. Those who cannot must be helped.

206888.explanation: Who supervises? Who opens/closes the building? Every school must be free and selective in whether and to whom they make the building available. The municipality should not impose obligations on this.

206889.explanation: We already have few municipal monuments, but we must cherish what we have, even if it costs more money.

206890.explanation: And that person must above all be visibly present and approachable for everyone. He/she should not be sitting in meetings in a neighborhood building.

206891.explanation: The lack of enforcement is a major concern. Enforcement is necessary, always, so if necessary 24/7.

206892.explanation: In the Netherlands, we have constitutional freedom of religion. Enforcement officers must also be allowed to express Christian forms of expression. The condition is that this must not hinder the execution of their work.

206893.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) must be enabled to do their work well and that perhaps requires more than just a baton.

206894.explanation: Not more camera surveillance, but the possibility to deploy it. We see much more value in visible enforcement on the street.

206896.explanation: The municipality is not a bank. The municipality must facilitate housing construction.

206897.explanation: Elderly people are willing to move and in many cases can easily afford it themselves. The municipality must mainly ensure sufficient housing construction 'across the board.' Then the flow will start naturally.

206898.explanation: We must build what is needed, and that might be more or less than 33%. But above all, we must build!

206899.explanation: We must be very careful when granting priority. Doing so always disadvantages others.

206900.explanation: We need to build what is necessary, which may be more or less than 25%. But above all, we must build!

206901.explanation: We must not lock ourselves up. Where green spaces might have to give way, we can compensate elsewhere. Always seeking balance. But building must happen.

206903.explanation: Not more money, we already spend enough on that. A much greater societal evil is undermining and drug crime. We can achieve much more socially there.

206904.explanation: That would send a very wrong signal. People with debt problems must be properly and sustainably helped, which requires tailor-made solutions.

206907.explanation: Good provisions exist for low-income groups. Let's maintain those and adjust them where necessary.

206908.explanation: We believe they are sufficiently available. New developments may require new parking spaces. This demands tailored solutions.

206910.explanation: Preferably entirely car-free, with the remark that parking garages must remain accessible.

206911.explanation: A tram would add nothing to public transport in Eindhoven. We have the high-quality public transport (HOV) with separated bus lanes, and we should continue to expand that.

206912.explanation: 70 km/h with as much traffic light coordination as possible. Eventually, all intersections on the Ring Road should be grade-separated.

206913.explanation: We must introduce (and enforce) 30 km/h zones where necessary, but not everywhere.

206914.explanation: Many people can pay that quite easily. For those who need it and cannot pay, we must provide tailored support.

206915.explanation: Sounds sympathetic, but is unenforceable. So we should not want that.

206856.explanation: EVE agrees with this. Tech companies create the prosperity and jobs of the future, and Eindhoven plays an important role in that. At the same time, new companies must grow responsibly so that economic development goes hand in hand with livability, affordability, and quality of life.

206857.explanation: ASML is of great value for tomorrow's prosperity and for the region. At the same time, the municipality should not focus on the growth of a single company but on a healthy economy and prosperous society where companies can thrive and contribute their fair share.

206858.explanation: Nuclear energy is a complex national issue. Eindhoven is not the appropriate authority to decide on this.

206859.explanation: EVE finds participation essential. Seniors but also youths, for example, must be well involved in policymaking. Through various forms of participation, we involve residents in thinking along. Council participation occurs via lottery so everyone has equal chances and representation is representative.

206860.explanation: EVE sees the citizens' assembly as one of the most important tools to give residents real influence in municipal policy. When carefully organized, it brings together a representative group of Eindhoven residents who work on widely supported solutions, thereby strengthening local democracy.

206861.explanation: EVE supports residents' say but does not see the referendum as the right instrument. It offers only a yes/no choice without room for nuance, dialogue, or joint consideration and increases the risk of narrowing debate and polarization.

206862.explanation: EVE supports making neighborhood budgets available so residents can improve their neighborhood. This is already happening, but we want to strengthen it. Good guidance, clear information, and equal support are needed so every neighborhood can benefit.

206863.explanation: EVE believes that the property tax (OZB) should, in principle, not rise faster than inflation to keep housing costs manageable for the average resident. At the same time, we think more differentiation is needed so that wealthier parties contribute more than less wealthy residents.

206864.explanation: EVE considers sustainability important and expects companies to commit to climate and livability. A strict admission requirement is unrealistic. We choose to encourage and impose realistic obligations with clear standards and enforcement so that Eindhoven becomes more sustainable without harming economic opportunities.

206865.explanation: Eindhoven is already an attractive location thanks to strong city marketing. Additional promotion budgets are therefore not self-evident. EVE wants municipal money to go primarily to livability, facilities, and space for residents. A good city for residents is also attractive to companies.

206866.explanation: Philips is strongly connected to Eindhoven. The return of its headquarters could strengthen employment, knowledge, and reputation. The municipality may commit to this, provided it happens under reasonable conditions, balanced with the city's interest, and only if Philips itself wants this.

206867.explanation: EVE supports abolishing the dog tax. It is not a significant source of income and mainly affects people with lower incomes. For the elderly and singles, a dog provides companionship and helps against loneliness.

206868.explanation: EVE believes it is important that entrepreneurs can do business, but full freedom in opening hours leads to unfair competition and extra pressure on employees. Joint agreements ensure fair competition, good working conditions, and livable neighborhoods.

206869.explanation: EVE finds sustainability important but questions whether it is the municipality's role to determine which products are morally desirable through advertising bans. The government should set clear frameworks and encourage sustainable choices, but also leave residents and entrepreneurs room for their own choices.

206870.explanation: EVE advocates humane and careful reception of asylum seekers. Distribution throughout the city prevents too much pressure on neighborhoods and increases support and integration. Small-scale reception with clear agreements and good support is essential.

206871.explanation: EVE believes status holders have the right to good housing and can sometimes be given priority, but not automatically and not only in social rental housing. We choose a balanced approach with extra homes so that no one is played off against another and the housing shortage is solved rather than shifted.

206872.explanation: EVE finds investing in the integration of newcomers essential. Good guidance in language, work, education, and neighborhood participation ensures they become independent and can participate more quickly. This benefits both newcomers and Eindhoven.

206873.explanation: Housing more refugees than the legally required number is not a goal in itself. It depends on the context. In times of crisis, it could be more, and in other cases, another number may be appropriate. Quality, distribution, and solidarity are most important.

206874.explanation: The most important thing is that the municipality continues to pay attention to the slavery and colonial past in close consultation with descendants, aiming for recognition, impact, and connection. From this follows whether a step like this is logical.

206875.explanation: EVE stands for a clear separation between church and state. However, a quiet room for anyone who needs it can be a nice addition. This can also be used for non-religious reasons.

206876.explanation: EVE believes economic growth must not come at the expense of livability and housing. Labor migrants must be protected against exploitation and poor housing. More regulation is needed.

206877.explanation: De Hurk is located next to residential neighborhoods. EVE finds structural nuisance and health risks unacceptable, but solutions must be careful: no passing on problems, sustainability where possible, and attention to workers and livability.

206878.explanation: EVE believes greening neighborhoods contributes to livability, health, and climate resilience. By financially supporting residents in greening their neighborhood, we strengthen their involvement. The municipality must actively promote this with good guidance so all neighborhoods benefit.

206879.explanation: EVE wonders whether we need to have an opinion on this. For many residents, fishing is a relaxing activity.

206880.explanation: EVE supports a lively nightlife and sees room for extended opening hours. This can only happen under clear conditions, with attention to livability, safety, and night rest. Tailored approaches per area, good enforcement, and clear agreements are essential.

206881.explanation: EVE believes festivals and parties contribute to Eindhoven's vibrancy and connection and should be easier to organize. The municipality must more actively support initiators with guidance, clear procedures, and suitable locations, with attention to safety and livability.

206882.explanation: EVE sees municipal loans for sustainability as part of a urban development company. This way, the municipality can actively invest in sustainability, housing, and work. Loans are purposeful and revolving, so public money yields local returns.

206883.explanation: EVE believes that monuments should be better able to be made more sustainable without losing their historical value. Rigid rules make this difficult now. With smarter customization, monuments can become more energy-efficient and be preserved for the future.

206884.explanation: EVE believes that scarce land in Eindhoven should primarily be used for housing and facilities. Large solar parks do not fit with this. We choose solar panels on roofs, facades, and business parks, so the energy transition does not come at the expense of livability and greenery.

206885.explanation: EVE believes that everyone should be able to participate in sports, regardless of income. The municipality can broaden and improve accessibility by targeted co-funding of sports memberships. This way, we ensure equal opportunities, better health, and more social connection.

206886.explanation: EVE believes that neighborhood libraries are indispensable as low-threshold meeting places that offer much more than just books. This is especially important for livable neighborhoods. Each district already has one, but expansion is possible.

206887.explanation: EVE believes that the library should be freely accessible to everyone. Knowledge and culture should not depend on income. A free library lowers barriers and contributes to education, participation, and equal opportunities, which justifies public funding.

206888.explanation: EVE believes school buildings should also serve a function for the neighborhood. By opening them outside school hours to clubs and organizations, we make better use of space and strengthen meeting and cohesion, provided this is well and safely organized.

206889.explanation: EVE believes municipal monuments are an important part of Eindhoven's identity and history. By investing in protection and maintenance, this heritage remains preserved and can be used sustainably and actively in the city and neighborhoods.

206890.explanation: EVE believes safety and livability start in the neighborhood. A dedicated community enforcement officer (boa) per district ensures recognizability and trust, short lines with residents, and more effective tackling of nuisance. This makes enforcement more humane and neighborhood-focused.

206891.explanation: EVE believes that community enforcement officers (boa’s) must be flexibly deployable for safety and livability, including weekdays and nights. This should not become a standard 24/7 pressure. Deployment must be targeted and proportional, coordinated with the police and with attention to good working conditions.

206892.explanation: EVE believes that community enforcement officers (boa’s) should have a neutral appearance. Religious expressions do not belong on government uniforms. This way, enforcers are recognizable and impartial to everyone and can perform their work authoritatively and connectively.

206893.explanation: EVE believes community enforcement officers (boa’s) must be able to work safely, but we are unsure if the baton is the solution. For this reason, we support a pilot to examine whether a baton adds value.

206894.explanation: EVE believes safety is important. Cameras can help at specific problem locations but are not a standard tool. Privacy and proportionality come first. Prevention, visible enforcement officers, and strong neighborhoods remain essential, though camera surveillance can make public spaces safer.

206896.explanation: EVE believes that first-time buyers with low or middle incomes can receive support, but only targeted and supplementary. Generic loans drive prices up. The real solution lies in building enough affordable homes: the housing shortage must be solved, not shifted.

206897.explanation: EVE wants to stimulate movement in the housing market. A relocation premium for seniors can help free up larger homes for families. This premium should be targeted and temporary, used via a city development company, provided there are sufficient suitable homes for seniors.

206898.explanation: EVE believes new construction should pay more attention to life-course suitable homes and ease of movement for seniors. They are ultimately the largest target group in Eindhoven.

206899.explanation: EVE believes Eindhoven residents should have a fair chance at social rental housing in their own city. By prioritizing local housing seekers during allocation, we strengthen ties to the city and limit local housing shortages, within legal frameworks and with attention to vulnerable groups.

206900.explanation: EVE believes young people have their own place on the housing market and that there are too few suitable homes for them. Social rental housing is broader and often too expensive for students. We opt for customization: targeted youth housing and affordable living forms, alongside sufficient social rental for everyone.

206901.explanation: EVE believes the housing shortage is great and more building is needed. This may, under strict conditions, come at the expense of greenery, provided it is compensated elsewhere. Green remains essential. That is why we choose smart densification, building within the city, and multiple use of space.

206903.explanation: EVE believes the municipality should work from trust instead of distrust. Less control gives people on social assistance space towards work or education and allows civil servants more time for guidance. This is more humane, effective, and efficient.

206904.explanation: EVE believes people with benefits and problematic debts deserve perspective. By targeted debt repayment and partial forgiveness, they gain peace and space to participate again, with good guidance towards work or education.

206907.explanation: EVE believes mobility must not be a luxury. People with little money must be able to keep moving. Free public transport prevents exclusion, increases self-reliance, and helps people participate actively in society.

206908.explanation: EVE believes Eindhoven must be accessible for everyone. Extra parking spaces for people with disabilities are necessary to enable independent participation. By investing in this, we ensure equal opportunities, better accessibility, and an inclusive city.

206910.explanation: EVE believes a car-reduced city center contributes to livability and safety. Fewer cars result in cleaner air and more space for pedestrians and cyclists, provided this is done carefully and phased with good accessibility for residents, entrepreneurs, and people with disabilities.

206911.explanation: EVE believes Eindhoven should invest in high-quality public transport. A tram improves accessibility, reduces car traffic, and supports sustainable growth. Investing now prevents higher costs and delays in the future.

206912.explanation: EVE believes the maximum speed on the Eindhoven ring road should remain 70 km/h. This is a good balance between traffic flow, safety, and livability. Further reduction only with demonstrable necessity to maintain the ring road's accessible function.

206913.explanation: EVE believes residential neighborhoods are living areas, not through roads. A maximum speed of 30 km/h increases traffic safety, reduces noise nuisance, and makes streets safer for children, elderly, and cyclists.

206914.explanation: EVE believes protection against extreme heat is important, especially for vulnerable elderly. However, we think financial support should go to seniors who want air conditioning but cannot afford it.

206915.explanation: EVE considers informal caregivers indispensable and wants them to be able to provide care without extra obstacles. Free parking near the care location can help, provided this is arranged in a targeted and careful way via an informal care scheme to prevent misuse.

shootoutStatements.title: Each neighborhood in Eindhoven should receive a fixed annual sum that residents can use to improve their neighborhood.
Eindhoven, together with Veldhoven, should fully support the growth of high-tech company ASML.
The municipality should ban advertising in public spaces for products that are not sustainable.
The property tax (ozb) may not rise faster than inflation in the Netherlands.
The municipality should allow the construction of a small nuclear reactor.
Eindhoven should apologize for its colonial and slavery past.
The municipality should invest extra money in a promotional campaign for Eindhoven as an attractive location for businesses.
The municipality should regularly organize a citizens' assembly that gives advice to the municipal council. A citizens' assembly consists of residents chosen by lot.
Asylum seekers should be accommodated across the city of Eindhoven.
The municipality should only cooperate with the arrival of new companies if housing for migrant workers is properly arranged.
The municipality should provide funding for residents who want more shrubs and plants in their neighborhood.
The rules for making listed buildings more sustainable should be made more flexible.
Of all new homes to be built in Eindhoven, at least one third should be suitable for people aged 50 and over.
Companies that want to become more sustainable should be able to get a loan from the municipality for that.
The municipality should contribute to sports club memberships for low-income people.
The municipality should not make land available in Eindhoven for large solar parks.
The municipality should try to bring Philips' headquarters back to Eindhoven.
The municipality should create more parking spaces for people with disabilities.
Each neighborhood in the municipality of Eindhoven should get its own library.
Eindhoven should expand public transport by adding a tram.
School buildings should be open in the evenings and at weekends for clubs and community organizations.
Each neighborhood of Eindhoven should have a permanent enforcement officer (boa).
Municipal enforcement officers (boa's) should be able to be deployed 24 hours a day across Eindhoven, including on weekdays.
At least 25% of all new social rental homes should go to young people.
Low-income people should be allowed to continue using public transport for free in Eindhoven.
